```{r both years psychosocial model A stats table build, include=TRUE}
dfkruskalTestTable[6,4] <- empathy_kruskal[4,5]
dfkruskalTestTable[6,5] <- selfesteem_kruskal[4,3]
dfkruskalTestTable[6,6] <- selfesteem_kruskal[4,5]
dfkruskalTestTable[7,3] <- empathy_kruskal[5,3]
dfkruskalTestTable[7,4] <- empathy_kruskal[5,5]
dfkruskalTestTable[7,5] <- selfesteem_kruskal[5,3]
dfkruskalTestTable[7,6] <- selfesteem_kruskal[5,5]
dfkruskalTestTable[11,3] <- empathy_kruskal[7,3]
dfkruskalTestTable[11,4] <- empathy_kruskal[7,5]
dfkruskalTestTable[11,5] <- selfesteem_kruskal[7,3]
dfkruskalTestTable[11,6] <- selfesteem_kruskal[7,5]
dfkruskalTestTable[12,3] <- empathy_kruskal[8,3]
dfkruskalTestTable[12,4] <- empathy_kruskal[8,5]
dfkruskalTestTable[12,5] <- selfesteem_kruskal[8,3]
dfkruskalTestTable[12,6] <- selfesteem_kruskal[8,5]
dfkruskalTestTable[13,3] <- empathy_kruskal[9,3]
dfkruskalTestTable[13,4] <- empathy_kruskal[9,5]
dfkruskalTestTable[13,5] <- selfesteem_kruskal[9,3]
dfkruskalTestTable[13,6] <- selfesteem_kruskal[9,5]


dfkruskalTestTable[2:4,3] <- empathy_dunn[1:3,4]
dfkruskalTestTable[2:4,4] <- empathy_dunn[1:3,6]
dfkruskalTestTable[2:4,5] <- selfesteem_dunn[1:3,4]
dfkruskalTestTable[2:4,6] <- selfesteem_dunn[1:3,6]

dfkruskalTestTable[8:10,3] <- empathy_dunn[4:6,4]
dfkruskalTestTable[8:10,4] <- empathy_dunn[4:6,6]

dfkruskalTestTable[8:10,5] <- selfesteem_dunn[4:6,4]
dfkruskalTestTable[8:10,6] <- selfesteem_dunn[4:6,6]

dfkruskalTestTable[14:16,3] <- empathy_dunn[7:9,4]
dfkruskalTestTable[14:16,4] <- empathy_dunn[7:9,6]

dfkruskalTestTable[14:16,5] <- selfesteem_dunn[7:9,4]
dfkruskalTestTable[14:16,6] <- selfesteem_dunn[7:9,6]

dfkruskalTestTable[17:22,3] <- empathy_dunn[10:15,4]
dfkruskalTestTable[17:22,4] <- empathy_dunn[10:15,6]

dfkruskalTestTable[17:22,5] <- selfesteem_dunn[10:15,4]
dfkruskalTestTable[17:22,6] <- selfesteem_dunn[10:15,6]

## create flextables from dataframe
set_flextable_defaults(theme_fun = theme_booktabs)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- flextable(dfkruskalTestTable)

## set format of flextable
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- autofit(flexdfkruskalTestTable)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- height(flexdfkruskalTestTable, height=0.08)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- width(flexdfkruskalTestTable, j=1, 1)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- width(flexdfkruskalTestTable, j=2, 2)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- width(flexdfkruskalTestTable, j=3, .7)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- width(flexdfkruskalTestTable, j=4, .7)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- width(flexdfkruskalTestTable, j=5, .7)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- width(flexdfkruskalTestTable, j=6, .7)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- fontsize(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i = NULL, j = NULL, size = 9, part = "all")
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=1)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=4)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=5)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=6)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=7)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=10)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=11)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=12)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=13)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=16)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- bg(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=3, j=3:6, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- bg(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=9, j=3:6, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- bg(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=15, j=3:6, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- bg(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=18, j=3:6, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- bg(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=20, j=3:6, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- bg(flexdfkruskalTestTable, i=22, j=3:6, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- bold(flexdfkruskalTestTable, ~  (`Empathy Year 1 p value` < .05) & (`Empathy Year 2 p value` < .05), bold=TRUE)
flexdfkruskalTestTable <- bold(flexdfkruskalTestTable, ~  (`Self-Esteem Year 1 p value` < .05) | (`Self-Esteem Year 2 p value` < .05), bold = TRUE)
flexdfkruskalTestTable

```

<br/>

#### Model B	Do school resilience indicators predict student outcomes of EGRA, EGMA, and hope?

Model A includes student perceptions of themselves as predictors of each outcome (EGRA normalized gains, EGMA normalized gain, and hope). The independent student perceptions for model A include: school benefit, safety at school, and teacher equity.

**Retained Student Correlation Heat Map**
```{r model B correlation plots, include=TRUE, width = 7.5}
rquery.cormatMethod(EndlinestudentsurveyModelCCorr)
```

<br/>

##### Student Perception of School Benefit

The School Benefit scale items were used to assess student School Benefit while being at school. The scale is composed of the average of the following 6 items: 

<br/>

 * My school activities helped me learn to work well with other children.
 * My school activities helped me learn to read and do math.
 * My school activities made me more confident in my ability to learn.
 * My school activities made me feel more positive about achieving my goals.
 * My school activities helped me think about new opportunities in life.
 * I am happy with my school.


<br/>

All items were collected on a 5 point Likert scale(0-strongly disagree, 1-disagree, 2-neither agree nor disagree, 3-agree, 4-strongly agree) and they were converted to a 6 point scale (0-strongly disagree, 1.25-disagree, 2.5-neither agree nor disagree,  3.75-agree, 5-strongly agree) where students indicated their School Benefit during the past week.   

```{r student benefit or student satisfaction midline to endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item
stdntbenefit_avg_flex <- build_meanEndline3_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MStdntSatisCNV_Avg, EL2StdntSatisCNV_Avg, "School Benefit Perceptions")

```

`r stdntbenefit_avg_flex`

<br/>

##### Student Perception of Safety at School at Endline


<br/>

All items were measured on a 5 point Likert scale (0-none of the time, 1-a little of the time, 2-some of the time, 3-a lot of the time, 4-most of the time, 5- all of the time) where students indicated their level of difficulty learning when feeling unsafe at school.

```{r safety at school midline to endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item
safetyschool_avg_Main_flex <- build_meanEndline_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2SafetySchool_Avg, "Retained Difficulty learning when feeling unsafe at school")
                                                                         
```

`r safetyschool_avg_Main_flex`


<br/>

##### Student Perception of Teacher Equity 

The Equity scale items were used to assess student perception of equity in the classroom.  The scale is composed of the average of the following 2 items:

<br/>

  * My teacher treats me fairly at school.
  * In my classroom, all children are treated the same.
  * In my classroom, all children are treated fairly.
  
<br/>

All items were measured on 6 point Likert scale (0-None of the time, 1-A little of the time, 2-Some of the time, 3-A lot of the time, 4-Most of the time, 5-All the time) where students indicated the frequency of perceived equity with each statement.

```{r equity midline to endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item
equity_avg_flex <- build_meanEndline3_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MEquity_Avg, EL2Equity_Avg, "Student Perceptions of Teacher Equity")

```


`r equity_avg_flex`

<br/>

#### Model B Variances by Demographics

Student perceptions of school benefit, student perception of teacher equity, and the difficulty in learning while feeling unsafe at school were all analyzed for variances among the demographic subgroups.  The table below shows the p values when checking for significance of those variances. Demographic categories with only two subgroups have one line in the table representing the significance of the variance between those two subgroups and demographic categories with more than two subgroups have more than one line, with each line representing the significance of the variance between each of the subgroup groupings. A line in the table is bolded if any of the p values represent a significant variance.  In the case of more than two subgroups, the p value displayed is the adjusted p value using a Bonferroni correction.

```{r both years psychosocial model B stats table build, include=TRUE}
dfkruskalModelBTable <- as.data.frame(matrix(nrow=22, ncol = 7))
colnames(dfkruskalModelBTable) <- c("Group", "Subgroup", "Benefit Year 1 p value", "Benefit Year 2 p value", "Equity Year 1 p value", "Equity Year 2 p value", "School Safety Diff Learn Year 2 p value")

## Set row labels 
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[1] <- "Program Type"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[2] <- "Location Type"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[5] <- "IDP Family"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[6] <- "Gender"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[7] <- "Age Group"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[8] <- "Socio-Economic Status"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[11] <- "Mother's Literacy"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[12] <- "Father's Literacy"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[13] <- "Lives without Parents"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[14] <- "Dialect Spoken"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Group[17] <- "State"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[1] <- "BAB - Formal Primary"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[2] <- "IDP - Rural"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[3] <- "IDP - Urban"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[4] <- "Rural - Urban"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[5] <- "Not IDP Family - IDP Family"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[6] <- "Male - Female"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[7] <- "4 to 11 year olds - 12 to 20 year olds"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[8] <- "high - low"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[9] <- "high - medium"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[10] <- "low - medium"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[11] <- "no - yes"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[12] <- "no - yes"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[13] <- "no - yes"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[14] <- "Maay - Maxaa tiri"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[15] <- "Maay - Other"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[16] <- "Maxaa tiri - Other"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[17] <- "Benaadir - Hirshabeelle"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[18] <- "Benaadir - Jubbaland"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[19] <- "Hirshabeelle - Jubbaland"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[20] <- "Benaadir - South-West"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[21] <- "Hirshabeelle - South-West"
dfkruskalModelBTable$Subgroup[22] <- "Jubbaland - South-West"


schoolbenefit_kruskal <- build_kruskalEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MStdntSatisCNV_Avg, EL2StdntSatisCNV_Avg, 'School Benefit Kruskal')

schoolbenefit_dunn <- build_dunnEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MStdntSatisCNV_Avg, EL2StdntSatisCNV_Avg, 'School Benefit Dunn')

equity_kruskal <- build_kruskalEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MEquity_Avg, EL2Equity_Avg, 'Equity Kruskal')

equity_dunn <- build_dunnEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MEquity_Avg, EL2Equity_Avg, 'Equity Dunn')

safetyschool_kruskal <- build_kruskalMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2SafetySchool_Avg, 'School Safety Kruskal')

safetyschool_dunn <- build_dunnMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2SafetySchool_Avg, 'School Safety Dunn')

dfkruskalModelBTable[1,3]  <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[1,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[1,4] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[1,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[1,5] <- equity_kruskal[1,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[1,6] <- equity_kruskal[1,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[1,7] <- safetyschool_kruskal[1,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[5,3] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[3,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[5,4] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[3,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[5,5] <- equity_kruskal[3,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[5,6] <- equity_kruskal[3,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[5,7] <- safetyschool_kruskal[3,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[6,3] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[4,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[6,4] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[4,5]

dfkruskalModelBTable[6,6] <- equity_kruskal[4,5]

dfkruskalModelBTable[7,3] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[5,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[7,4] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[5,5]

dfkruskalModelBTable[7,6] <- equity_kruskal[5,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[7,7] <- safetyschool_kruskal[5,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[11,3] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[7,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[11,4] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[7,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[11,5] <- equity_kruskal[7,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[11,6] <- equity_kruskal[7,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[11,7] <- safetyschool_kruskal[7,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[12,3] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[8,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[12,4] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[8,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[12,5] <- equity_kruskal[8,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[12,6] <- equity_kruskal[8,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[12,7] <- safetyschool_kruskal[8,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[13,3] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[9,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[13,4] <- schoolbenefit_kruskal[9,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[13,5] <- equity_kruskal[9,3]
dfkruskalModelBTable[13,6] <- equity_kruskal[9,5]
dfkruskalModelBTable[13,7] <- safetyschool_kruskal[9,3]

dfkruskalModelBTable[2:4,3] <- schoolbenefit_dunn[1:3,4]
dfkruskalModelBTable[2:4,4] <- schoolbenefit_dunn[1:3,6]
dfkruskalModelBTable[2:4,5] <- equity_dunn[1:3,4]
dfkruskalModelBTable[2:4,6] <- equity_dunn[1:3,6]
dfkruskalModelBTable[2:4,7] <- safetyschool_dunn[1:3,4]
dfkruskalModelBTable[8:10,3] <- schoolbenefit_dunn[4:6,4]
dfkruskalModelBTable[8:10,4] <- schoolbenefit_dunn[4:6,6]
dfkruskalModelBTable[8:10,5] <- equity_dunn[4:6,4]
dfkruskalModelBTable[8:10,6] <- equity_dunn[4:6,6]
dfkruskalModelBTable[8:10,7] <- safetyschool_dunn[4:6,4]

dfkruskalModelBTable[14:16,5] <- equity_dunn[7:9,4]

dfkruskalModelBTable[14:16,7] <- safetyschool_dunn[7:9,4]
dfkruskalModelBTable[17:22,3] <- schoolbenefit_dunn[10:15,4]

dfkruskalModelBTable[17:22,7] <- safetyschool_dunn[10:15,4]


## create flextables from dataframe
set_flextable_defaults(theme_fun = theme_booktabs)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- flextable(dfkruskalModelBTable)

## set format of flextable
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- autofit(flexdfkruskalModelBTable)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- height(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, height=0.08)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- width(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, j=1, 1)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- width(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, j=2, 2)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- width(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, j=3, .7)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- width(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, j=4, .7)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- width(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, j=5, .7)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- width(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, j=6, .7)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- width(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, j=7, 1)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- fontsize(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, i = NULL, j = NULL, size = 9, part = "all")
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, i=1)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- hline(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, i=4)

flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- bg(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, i=22, j=3:7, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- bold(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, ~  (`Benefit Year 1 p value` < .05) & (`Benefit Year 2 p value` < .05), bold=TRUE)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- bold(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, ~  (`Equity Year 1 p value` < .05) | (`Equity Year 2 p value` < .05), bold = TRUE)
flexdfkruskalModelBTable <- bold(flexdfkruskalModelBTable, ~  (`School Safety Diff Learn Year 2 p value` < .05), bold = TRUE)

flexdfkruskalModelBTable
```

<br/>

#### Model C	Do Community Resilience indicators predict EGRA, EGMA, and hope?

Model A includes student perceptions of themselves as predictors of each outcome (EGRA normalized gains, EGMA normalized gain, and hope). The independent student perceptions for model A include: community safety, home safety, civic engagement, and community belonging.

**Retained Student Correlation Heat Map**
```{r model C correlation plots, include=TRUE, width = 7.5}
rquery.cormatMethod(EndlinestudentsurveyModelDCorr)
```

##### Student Perception of Safety in the Community at Endline


All items were measured on a 5 point Likert scale (0-none of the time, 1-a little of the time, 2-some of the time, 3-a lot of the time, 4-most of the time, 5- all of the time) where students indicated their level of difficulty learning when feeling unsafe in the community.

```{r safety at school at endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item
safetycmmnty_avg_Main_flex <- build_meanEndline_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2SafetyCmmnty_Avg, "Retained Difficulty learning when feeling unsafe in the community")
                                                                         
```

`r safetycmmnty_avg_Main_flex`


<br/>

##### Student Perception of Safety at Home at Endline


All items were measured on a 5 point Likert scale (0-none of the time, 1-a little of the time, 2-some of the time, 3-a lot of the time, 4-most of the time, 5- all of the time) where students indicated their level of difficulty learning when feeling unsafe at home.  

```{r safety at home at endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item
safetyhome_avg_Main_flex <- build_meanEndline_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2SafetyHome_Avg, "Retained Difficulty learning when feeling unsafe at home")
                                                                         
```


`r safetyhome_avg_Main_flex`

<br/>

##### Student Perception of Civic Engagement at Endline

The Civic Engagement scale items were used to assess student Civic engagement while being at school. Scale items derive from the School Engagement Scale (Fredericks, et.al., 2005). The scale is composed of the average of the following 3 items: 

<br/>

 * I feel responsible for my community.
 * I believe I should make a difference in my community.
 * I believe I should help others in my community.

<br/>

All items were measured on a 6 point Likert scale (0-None of the time, 1-A little of the time, 2-Some of the time, 3-A lot of the time, 4-Most of the time, 5-All the time) where students indicated their Civic engagement during the past week.   

```{r civic engagement midline to endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item
civicengage_avg_flex <- build_meanEndline3_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MCivicEngage_Avg, EL2CivicEngage_Avg, "Civic Engagement Perceptions")
                                                                         
```

`r civicengage_avg_flex`

<br/>

##### Student Perception of Community Belonging at Endline


All items were measured on a 5 point Likert scale (0-strongly disagree, .83-disagree, 1.67-somewhat disagree, 2.5-neither agree nor disagree, 3.33-somewhat agree, 4.17-agree, 5-strongly agree) where students indicated their School Benefit during the past week.   

```{r community belong at endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item
cmmntyblngng_avg_Main_flex <- build_meanEndline_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2CmmntyBlngngCNV_Avg, "Retained Student Perceptions of Community Belonging\n")
                                                                
```

`r cmmntyblngng_avg_Main_flex`

<br/>


#### Model C Variances by Demographics

Student perceptions of civic engagement, community belonging, and the difficulty in learning while feeling unsafe at home and in the community were all analyzed for variances among the demographic subgroups.  The table below shows the p values when checking for significance of those variances. Demographic categories with only two subgroups have one line in the table representing the significance of the variance between those two subgroups and demographic categories with more than two subgroups have more than one line, with each line representing the significance of the variance between each of the subgroup groupings. A line in the table is bolded if any of the p values represent a significant variance.  In the case of more than two subgroups, the p value displayed is the adjusted p value using a Bonferroni correction.

```{r both years psychosocial model C stats table build, include=TRUE}
dfkruskalModelCTable <- as.data.frame(matrix(nrow=22, ncol = 7))
colnames(dfkruskalModelCTable) <- c("Group", "Subgroup", "Civic Engage Year 1 p value", "Civic Engage Year 2 p value", "Cmmnty Blngng Year 2 p value", "Home Safety Diff Learn Year 2 p value", "Cmmnty Safety Diff Learn Year 2 p value")

## Set row labels 
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[1] <- "Program Type"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[2] <- "Location Type"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[5] <- "IDP Family"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[6] <- "Gender"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[7] <- "Age Group"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[8] <- "Socio-Economic Status"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[11] <- "Mother's Literacy"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[12] <- "Father's Literacy"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[13] <- "Lives without Parents"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[14] <- "Dialect Spoken"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Group[17] <- "State"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[1] <- "BAB - Formal Primary"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[2] <- "IDP - Rural"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[3] <- "IDP - Urban"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[4] <- "Rural - Urban"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[5] <- "Not IDP Family - IDP Family"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[6] <- "Male - Female"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[7] <- "4 to 11 year olds - 12 to 20 year olds"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[8] <- "high - low"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[9] <- "high - medium"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[10] <- "low - medium"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[11] <- "no - yes"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[12] <- "no - yes"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[13] <- "no - yes"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[14] <- "Maay - Maxaa tiri"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[15] <- "Maay - Other"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[16] <- "Maxaa tiri - Other"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[17] <- "Benaadir - Hirshabeelle"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[18] <- "Benaadir - Jubbaland"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[19] <- "Hirshabeelle - Jubbaland"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[20] <- "Benaadir - South-West"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[21] <- "Hirshabeelle - South-West"
dfkruskalModelCTable$Subgroup[22] <- "Jubbaland - South-West"

civic_kruskal <- build_kruskalEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MCivicEngage_Avg, EL2CivicEngage_Avg, 'Civic Engage Kruskal')

civic_dunn <- build_dunnEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MCivicEngage_Avg, EL2CivicEngage_Avg, 'Civic Engage Dunn')

cmmntyblngng_kruskal <- build_kruskalMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2CmmntyBlngngCNV_Avg, 'Community Belonging')

cmmntyblngng_dunn <- build_dunnMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2CmmntyBlngngCNV_Avg, 'Community Belonging')

safetycmmnty_kruskal <- build_kruskalMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2SafetyCmmnty_Avg, 'Community Safety Kruskal')

safetycmmnty_dunn <- build_dunnMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2SafetyCmmnty_Avg, 'Community Safety Dunn')

safetyhome_kruskal <- build_kruskalMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2SafetyHome_Avg, 'Home Safety Kruskal')

safetyhome_dunn <- build_dunnMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2SafetyHome_Avg, 'Home Safety Dunn')

dfkruskalModelCTable[1,3] <- civic_kruskal[1,3]
dfkruskalModelCTable[1,4] <- civic_kruskal[1,5]

flexdfkruskalModelCTable <- flextable(dfkruskalModelCTable)

## set format of flextable
flexdfkruskalModelCTable <- autofit(flexdfkruskalModelCTable)
flexdfkruskalModelCTable <- height(flexdfkruskalModelCTable, height=0.08)

flexdfkruskalModelCTable <- bold(flexdfkruskalModelCTable, ~  (`Cmmnty Blngng Year 2 p value` < .05), bold = TRUE)

flexdfkruskalModelCTable
```

<br/>

#### Student Perception of Hope at Midline

The Hope domain can be divided into two subscales: agency and pathways. Agency promotes self-fulfillment, the ability to act on one’s own. Pathways explores multiple ways of accomplishing a task or goal. We have hope when agency and pathways are open to us. The 6 items included are:

<br/>
Agency:

 * I think I am doing pretty well.
 * I am doing just as well as other kids my age.
 * I think the things I’ve done in the past will help me in the future.

Pathways:

 * I can think of many ways to get the things in life that are most important to me.
 * When I have a problem, I can come up with many ways to solve it.
 * Even when others want to quit, I know that I can find ways to solve the problem.

<br/>

All items were measured on a 6 point Likert scale (0-None of the time, 1-A little of the time, 2-Some of the time, 3-A lot of the time, 4-Most of the time, 5-All of the time) where students indicated their perception of Hope.   

```{r hope midline to endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item

hope_avg_flex <- build_meanEndline3_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MHope_Avg, EL2Hope_Avg, "Student Perceptions of Hope")

HopeTtest <- t.test(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier$EL2Hope_Avg, EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier$MHope_Avg, paired = TRUE, alternative = "two.sided")
     
HopeTestTidy <- tidy(HopeTtest)
HopeTestTidyDf <- as.data.frame(HopeTestTidy)

## create flextables from dataframe
set_flextable_defaults(theme_fun = theme_box)
HopeTestFlex <- flextable(HopeTestTidyDf)
                                                          
```

`r hope_avg_flex`

<br/>

A T-test was used to compare the Hope Scale Average from the end of year 1 to the Hope Scale Average from the end of year 2.  The resulting statistics are seen below.  The resulting p value is well less than 0.00 and therefore 
the mean increase in Hope from year 1 to year 2 was statistically significant.

<br/>

`r HopeTestFlex`

<br/>


#### Overall Safety

The Overall safety scale items were used to assess general student safety in all three areas they spend time in. The scale is composed of the average of the following 3 items: 

<br/>

 * I feel safe at home.
 * I feel safe at school.
 * I feel safe in my community.
<br/>

All items were measured on a 6 point Likert scale (0-None of the time, 1-A little of the time, 2-Some of the time, 3-A lot of the time, 4-Most of the time, 5-All the time).  

```{r overall safety at endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item
safety_avg_Main_flex <- build_meanEndline_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2Safety_Avg, "Student Perceptions of Safety\n")
                                                                
```

<br/>

`r safety_avg_Main_flex`

<br/>

#### Responsibility Influence on Schoolwork

In order to measure the influence of responsibilities on schoolwork, we asked students how much chores influence their school attendance and homework.  The scale is composed of the average of the following 4 items: 

<br/>

 * How often do household chores make you miss school?
 * How often do household chores make it difficult to study?
 * How often do household chores make it difficult to finish homework?
 * How often do household chores make you late for school?
<br/>

All items were measured on a 6 point Likert scale (0-None of the time, 1-A little of the time, 2-Some of the time, 3-A lot of the time, 4-Most of the time, 5-All the time).  

```{r  at endline call mean function}
## call mean table functions build with equity avg scale item
chore_avg_Main_flex <- build_meanEndline_psychoSocial_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2CHR_INFLNC_Avg, "Responsibility Influence on Schoolwork\n")
```

<br/>

`r chore_avg_Main_flex`

<br/>

#### Student Perceptions of Socio-Economic Status (SES)

The SES scale items were used to assess student's perception of socio-economic status. Each item was a no=0 and yes=1 question with a higher value indicating a higher perceived SES level. The SES composite Score at baseline was composed of the summation of the following 3 items with a range of 0 to 3:

<br/>
  
  * Do you have electricity at home?
  * Do you have a radio at home?
  * Does your house have a telephone or mobile phone?

These three items were classified into 4 categories as follows: 

<br/>

   * 0 = none
   * 1 = low
   * 2 = medium
   * 3 = high
   
<br/>

At Endline the SES composite score was expanded and composed of the following 7 items:

  * Besides your schoolbooks, do you have other books at home for reading (story books, magazines, newspaper, etc)
  * Do you have electricity at home?
  * Do you have a radio at home?
  * Does your house have a separate bathroom/toilet?
  * Does your house have a telephone or mobile phone?
  * Does your family have access to clean water?
  * Do you have your own bed?

<br/>

The seven items at endline were further classified into three categories based on their quartiles as follows: 

  * a score of 0, 1, 2 = low
  * a score of 3, 4, 5 = medium
  * a score of 6, 7    = high
  
Year 1 in the table below represents the values at baseline and Year 2 represents the values at endline.  The baseline/Year 1 data was used in the student retention data and the endline/Year 2 data was used everywhere else in this data annex.
  
```{r SES boxplot for levels, include=TRUE}
boxplot(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier$EL2SES_Sum, col=c("steelblue"), xlab = "SES Endline", ylab="SES Composite", main="Box Plot Analysis\nEndline SES Composite Score")
```

```{r SES baseline to endline call mean, include=TRUE}

StatDF1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% dplyr::select('VarItem1'=BSES_Sum, EL2ProgramGrade_f, LocationTypeDesc_f, IDPFamily_f, Gender_f, AgeGroup1_f, MotherLiteracy_f, FatherLiteracy_f, StudentLivesWithOtherDesc, Languages2_f, State_f)

StatDF2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% dplyr::select('VarItem2'=EL2SES_Sum, EL2ProgramGrade_f, LocationTypeDesc_f, IDPFamily_f, Gender_f, AgeGroup1_f, MotherLiteracy_f, FatherLiteracy_f, StudentLivesWithOtherDesc, Languages2_f, State_f)

## Create data frame
SESMatcheddf <- as.data.frame(matrix(nrow = 25, ncol = 8))
## set dataframe column headers
colnames(SESMatcheddf) <- c("Group", "Subgroup", "Year 1 Mean", "Year 1 SD", "Year 1 p value", "Year 2 Mean", "Year 2 SD", "Year 2 p value")

## Retrieve Year 1 data for Program type
tempMeanProgramTypeYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(EL2ProgramGrade_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanProgramTypeYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanProgramTypeYr1)

## Retrieve Year 2 data for Pogram type
tempMeanProgramTypeYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(EL2ProgramGrade_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanProgramTypeYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanProgramTypeYr2)

## Retrieve n's for Program type
tempProgramN <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(EL2ProgramGrade_f) %>% count()
programLabel1 <- paste0(as.character(tempProgramN$EL2ProgramGrade_f[1]), " (n=", tempProgramN$n[1], ")")
programLabel2 <- paste0(as.character(tempProgramN$EL2ProgramGrade_f[2]), " (n=", tempProgramN$n[2], ")")

## Retrieve Year 1 Data for Location Type
tempMeanLocationTypeYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(LocationTypeDesc_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanLocationTypeYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanLocationTypeYr1)

## Retrieve Year 2 Data for Location Type
tempMeanLocationTypeYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(LocationTypeDesc_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanLocationTypeYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanLocationTypeYr2)

## Retrieve n's for Location Type and build label
tempLocationN <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(LocationTypeDesc_f) %>% count()
locationTypeLabel1 <- paste0(as.character(tempLocationN$LocationTypeDesc_f[1]), " (n=", tempLocationN$n[1], ")")
locationTypeLabel2 <- paste0(as.character(tempLocationN$LocationTypeDesc_f[2]), " (n=", tempLocationN$n[2], ")")
locationTypeLabel3 <- paste0(as.character(tempLocationN$LocationTypeDesc_f[3]), " (n=", tempLocationN$n[3], ")")

## Retrieve Year 1 data for IDP Family
tempMeanIDPFamilyYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(IDPFamily_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanIDPFamilyYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanIDPFamilyYr1)

## Retrieve Year 2 data for IDP Family
tempMeanIDPFamilyYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(IDPFamily_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanIDPFamilyYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanIDPFamilyYr2)

## Retrieve n's for IDP Family and build label
tempIDPFamilyN <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(IDPFamily_f) %>% count()
IDPFamilyLabel1 <- paste0(as.character(tempIDPFamilyN$IDPFamily_f[1]), " (n=", tempIDPFamilyN$n[1], ")")
IDPFamilyLabel2 <- paste0(as.character(tempIDPFamilyN$IDPFamily_f[2]), " (n=", tempIDPFamilyN$n[2], ")")

## Retrieve Year 1 Data for Gender
tempMeanGenderYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Gender_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanGenderYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanGenderYr1)

## Retrieve Year 2 Data for Gender
tempMeanGenderYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Gender_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanGenderYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanGenderYr2)

## Retrieve n's for Gender and build label
tempGenderN <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Gender_f) %>% count()
GenderLabel1 <- paste0(as.character(tempGenderN$Gender_f[1]), " (n=", tempGenderN$n[1], ")")
GenderLabel2 <- paste0(as.character(tempGenderN$Gender_f[2]), " (n=", tempGenderN$n[2], ")")

## Retrieve Year 1 Data for Age Group 1
tempMeanAgeGroup1Yr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(AgeGroup1_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanAgeGroup1Yr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanAgeGroup1Yr1)

## Retrieve Year 2 Data for Age Group 1
tempMeanAgeGroup1Yr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(AgeGroup1_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanAgeGroup1Yr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanAgeGroup1Yr2)

## Retrieve n's for AgeGroup1 and build label
tempAgeGroup1N <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(AgeGroup1_f) %>% count()
AgeGroup1Label1 <- paste0(as.character(tempAgeGroup1N$AgeGroup1_f[1]), " (n=", tempAgeGroup1N$n[1], ")")
AgeGroup1Label2 <- paste0(as.character(tempAgeGroup1N$AgeGroup1_f[2]), " (n=", tempAgeGroup1N$n[2], ")")
AgeGroup1Label3 <- paste0(as.character(tempAgeGroup1N$AgeGroup1_f[3]), " (n=", tempAgeGroup1N$n[3], ")")

## Retrieve Year 1 data for mother literacy
tempMeanMomLitYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(MotherLiteracy_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanMomLitYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanMomLitYr1)

## Retrieve Year 2 data for mother literacy
tempMeanMomLitYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(MotherLiteracy_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanMomLitYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanMomLitYr2)

## Retrieve n's for Mother Literacy and build label
tempMomLitN <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(MotherLiteracy_f) %>% count()
MomLitLabel1 <- paste0(as.character(tempMomLitN$MotherLiteracy_f[1]), " (n=", tempMomLitN$n[1], ")")
MomLitLabel2 <- paste0(as.character(tempMomLitN$MotherLiteracy_f[2]), " (n=", tempMomLitN$n[2], ")")

## Retrieve Year 1 data for father literacy
tempMeanDadLitYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(FatherLiteracy_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanDadLitYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanDadLitYr1)

## Retrieve Year 2 data for father literacy
tempMeanDadLitYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(FatherLiteracy_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanDadLitYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanDadLitYr2)

## Retrieve n's for Father Literacy and build label
tempDadLitN <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(FatherLiteracy_f) %>% count()
DadLitLabel1 <- paste0(as.character(tempDadLitN$FatherLiteracy_f[1]), " (n=", tempDadLitN$n[1], ")")
DadLitLabel2 <- paste0(as.character(tempDadLitN$FatherLiteracy_f[2]), " (n=", tempDadLitN$n[2], ")")

## Retrive Year1 data for lives with other
tempMeanLivesYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(StudentLivesWithOtherDesc) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanLivesYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanLivesYr1)

## Retrive Year2 data for lives with other
tempMeanLivesYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(StudentLivesWithOtherDesc) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanLivesYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanLivesYr2)

## Retrieve n's for Lives with Other and build label
tempLivesN <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(StudentLivesWithOtherDesc) %>% count()
LivesLabel1 <- paste0(as.character(tempLivesN$StudentLivesWithOtherDesc[1]), " (n=", tempLivesN$n[1], ")")
LivesLabel2 <- paste0(as.character(tempLivesN$StudentLivesWithOtherDesc[2]), " (n=", tempLivesN$n[2], ")")

## Retrieve Year 1 data for Languages
tempMeanLanguageYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanLanguageYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanLanguageYr1)

## Retrieve Year 2 data for Languages
tempMeanLanguageYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanLanguageYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanLanguageYr2)

## Retrieve n's for Languages and build label
tempLanguageN <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count()
LanguageLabel1 <- paste0(as.character(tempLanguageN$Languages2_f[1]), " (n=", tempLanguageN$n[1], ")")
LanguageLabel2 <- paste0(as.character(tempLanguageN$Languages2_f[2]), " (n=", tempLanguageN$n[2], ")")
LanguageLabel3 <- paste0(as.character(tempLanguageN$Languages2_f[3]), " (n=", tempLanguageN$n[3], ")")

## Retrive Year 1 Data for State
tempMeanStateYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(State_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanStateYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanStateYr1)

## Retrive Year 2 Data for State
tempMeanStateYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(State_f) %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempMeanStateYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempMeanStateYr2)

## Retrieve n's for State and build label
tempStateN <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(State_f) %>% count()
StateLabel1 <- paste0(as.character(tempStateN$State_f[1]), " (n=", tempStateN$n[1], ")")
StateLabel2 <- paste0(as.character(tempStateN$State_f[2]), " (n=", tempStateN$n[2], ")")
StateLabel3 <- paste0(as.character(tempStateN$State_f[3]), " (n=", tempStateN$n[3], ")")
StateLabel4 <- paste0(as.character(tempStateN$State_f[4]), " (n=", tempStateN$n[4], ")")

## Set row labels 
SESMatcheddf$Group[1] <- "SES Composite"
SESMatcheddf$Group[2] <- "Program Type"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[2] <- programLabel1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[3] <- programLabel2
SESMatcheddf$Group[4] <- "Location Type"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[4] <- locationTypeLabel1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[5] <- locationTypeLabel2
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[6] <- locationTypeLabel3
SESMatcheddf$Group[7] <- "IDP Family"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[7] <- IDPFamilyLabel1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[8] <- IDPFamilyLabel2
SESMatcheddf$Group[9] <- "Gender"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[9] <- GenderLabel1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[10] <- GenderLabel2
SESMatcheddf$Group[11] <- "Age Group"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[11] <- AgeGroup1Label1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[12] <- AgeGroup1Label2
SESMatcheddf$Group[13] <- "Mother's Literacy"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[13] <- MomLitLabel1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[14] <- MomLitLabel2
SESMatcheddf$Group[15] <- "Father's Literacy"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[15] <- DadLitLabel1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[16] <- DadLitLabel2
SESMatcheddf$Group[16] <- "Lives without Parents"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[16] <- LivesLabel1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[17] <- LivesLabel2
SESMatcheddf$Group[18] <- "Dialect Spoken"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[18] <- LanguageLabel1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[19] <- LanguageLabel2
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[20] <- LanguageLabel3
SESMatcheddf$Group[21] <- "State"
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[21] <- StateLabel1
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[22] <- StateLabel2
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[23] <- StateLabel3
SESMatcheddf$Subgroup[24] <- StateLabel4

## calculate base year 1 stats
tempMeanYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% summarise(mean = mean(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[1] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanYr1, sigdig)
tempsdYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% summarise(sd = sd(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempsdYr1)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[1] <- specify_decimal(tempsdYr1, sigdig)

## calculate base year 2 stats
tempMeanYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% summarise(mean = mean(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[1] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanYr2, sigdig)
tempsdYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% summarise(sd = sd(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempsdYr2)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[1] <- specify_decimal(tempsdYr2, sigdig)

## calculate Year 1 Program Type based stats(2-3) for mean_item_var1
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[2] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanProgramTypeYr1$mean[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[3] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanProgramTypeYr1$mean[2], sigdig)

## calculate Year 2 Program Type based stats(2-3) for mean_item_var2
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[2] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanProgramTypeYr2$mean[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[3] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanProgramTypeYr2$mean[2], sigdig)

## Calculate Year 1 Program Type SD for mean_item_var1
tempsdProgramTypeYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(EL2ProgramGrade_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdProgramTypeYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempsdProgramTypeYr1)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[2] <- specify_decimal(tempsdProgramTypeYr1$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[3] <- specify_decimal(tempsdProgramTypeYr1$sd[2], sigdig)

## Calculate Year 2 Program Type SD for mean_item_var2
tempsdProgramTypeYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(EL2ProgramGrade_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdProgramTypeYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempsdProgramTypeYr2)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[2] <- specify_decimal(tempsdProgramTypeYr2$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[3] <- specify_decimal(tempsdProgramTypeYr2$sd[2], sigdig)

## Check for normality and use appropriate test to calculate significant difference in Gains between progam types Year 1
ProgramTypeNormalityYr1 <- StatDF1 %>% 
	group_by(EL2ProgramGrade_f) %>% 
	summarise(`W Statistic` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$Statistic, 
	`p-value` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$p.value)
ProgramTypeNormalityYr1 <- as.data.frame(ProgramTypeNormalityYr1)
if (ProgramTypeNormalityYr1$`p-value`[1] > .05 && ProgramTypeNormalityYr1$`p-value`[2] > .05)
	{	
		onewayProgramTypeYr1 <- aov(VarItem1 ~ EL2ProgramGrade_f, data = StatDF1) 
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayProgramTypeYr1 <- tidy(onewayProgramTypeYr1)
		ProgramTypePvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayProgramTypeYr1$p.value[1]		
		} else
	{
		kruskalProgramTypeYr1 <- kruskal.test(VarItem1 ~ EL2ProgramGrade_f, data = StatDF1)
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalProgramTypeYr1 <- tidy(kruskalProgramTypeYr1)
		ProgramTypePvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalProgramTypeYr1$p.value[1]
	}	

SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 p value`[2] <- specify_decimal(ProgramTypePvalueYr1, sigdig5)

## Check for normality and use appropriate test to calculate significant difference in Gains between progam types Year 2
ProgramTypeNormalityYr2 <- StatDF2 %>% 
	group_by(EL2ProgramGrade_f) %>% 
	summarise(`W Statistic` = shapiro.test(VarItem2)$Statistic, 
	`p-value` = shapiro.test(VarItem2)$p.value)
ProgramTypeNormalityYr2 <- as.data.frame(ProgramTypeNormalityYr2)
if (ProgramTypeNormalityYr2$`p-value`[1] > .05 && ProgramTypeNormalityYr2$`p-value`[2] > .05)
	{	
		onewayProgramTypeYr2 <- aov(VarItem2 ~ EL2ProgramGrade_f, data = StatDF2) 
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayProgramTypeYr2 <- tidy(onewayProgramTypeYr2)
		ProgramTypePvalueYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayProgramTypeYr2$p.value[1]		
		} else
	{
		kruskalProgramTypeYr2 <- kruskal.test(VarItem2 ~ EL2ProgramGrade_f, data = StatDF2)
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalProgramTypeYr2 <- tidy(kruskalProgramTypeYr2)
		ProgramTypePvalueYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalProgramTypeYr2$p.value[1]
	}	

SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 p value`[2] <- specify_decimal(ProgramTypePvalueYr2, sigdig5)


## calculate Year 1 Location Type based stats(4-6) for mean_item_var1
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[4] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanLocationTypeYr1$mean[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[5] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanLocationTypeYr1$mean[2], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[6] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanLocationTypeYr1$mean[3], sigdig)

## calculate Year 2 Location Type based stats(4-6) for mean_item_var2
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[4] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanLocationTypeYr2$mean[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[5] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanLocationTypeYr2$mean[2], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[6] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanLocationTypeYr2$mean[3], sigdig)

## Calculate Year 1 Location Type SD for mean_item_var1
tempsdLocationTypeYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(LocationTypeDesc_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdLocationTypeYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempsdLocationTypeYr1)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[4] <- specify_decimal(tempsdLocationTypeYr1$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[5] <- specify_decimal(tempsdLocationTypeYr1$sd[2], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[6] <- specify_decimal(tempsdLocationTypeYr1$sd[3], sigdig)

## Calculate Year 2 Location Type SD for mean_item_var2
tempsdLocationTypeYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(LocationTypeDesc_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdLocationTypeYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempsdLocationTypeYr2)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[4] <- specify_decimal(tempsdLocationTypeYr2$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[5] <- specify_decimal(tempsdLocationTypeYr2$sd[2], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[6] <- specify_decimal(tempsdLocationTypeYr2$sd[3], sigdig)

## Check for normality Year 1 and use appropriate test to calculate significant difference in Gains between location types
LocationTypeNormalityYr1 <- StatDF1 %>% 
	group_by(LocationTypeDesc_f) %>% 
	summarise(`W Statistic` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$Statistic, 
	`p-value` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$p.value)
LocationTypeNormalityYr1 <- as.data.frame(LocationTypeNormalityYr1)
if (LocationTypeNormalityYr1$`p-value`[1] > .05 && LocationTypeNormalityYr1$`p-value`[2] > .05  && LocationTypeNormalityYr1$`p-value`[3] > .05)
	{	
		onewayLocationTypeYr1 <- aov(VarItem1 ~ LocationTypeDesc_f, data = StatDF1) 
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayLocationTypeYr1 <- tidy(onewayLocationTypeYr1)
		LocationTypePvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayLocationTypeYr1$p.value[1]		
		} else
	{
		kruskalLocationTypeYr1 <- kruskal.test(VarItem1 ~ LocationTypeDesc_f, data = StatDF1)
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalLocationTypeYr1 <- tidy(kruskalLocationTypeYr1)
		LocationTypePvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalLocationTypeYr1$p.value[1]
	}	

SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 p value`[4] <- specify_decimal(LocationTypePvalueYr1, sigdig5)

## Check for normality Year 2 and use appropriate test to calculate significant difference in Gains between location types
LocationTypeNormalityYr2 <- StatDF2 %>% 
	group_by(LocationTypeDesc_f) %>% 
	summarise(`W Statistic` = shapiro.test(VarItem2)$Statistic, 
	`p-value` = shapiro.test(VarItem2)$p.value)
LocationTypeNormalityYr2 <- as.data.frame(LocationTypeNormalityYr2)
if (LocationTypeNormalityYr2$`p-value`[1] > .05 && LocationTypeNormalityYr2$`p-value`[2] > .05  && LocationTypeNormalityYr2$`p-value`[3] > .05)
	{	
		onewayLocationTypeYr2 <- aov(VarItem2 ~ LocationTypeDesc_f, data = StatDF2) 
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayLocationTypeYr2 <- tidy(onewayLocationTypeYr2)
		LocationTypePvalueYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayLocationTypeYr2$p.value[1]		
		} else
	{
		kruskalLocationTypeYr2 <- kruskal.test(VarItem2 ~ LocationTypeDesc_f, data = StatDF2)
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalLocationTypeYr2 <- tidy(kruskalLocationTypeYr2)
		LocationTypePvalueYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalLocationTypeYr2$p.value[1]
	}	

SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 p value`[4] <- specify_decimal(LocationTypePvalueYr2, sigdig5)

## calculate midline IDP Family year 1 based stats(7-8) for mean_item_var1
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[7] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanIDPFamilyYr1$mean[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[8] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanIDPFamilyYr1$mean[2], sigdig)

## calculate endline IDP Family year 2 based stats(7-8) for mean_item_var2
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[7] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanIDPFamilyYr2$mean[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[8] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanIDPFamilyYr2$mean[2], sigdig)

## calculate midline IDP Family year 1 SD for mean_item_var1 (7-8)
tempsdIDPFamilyYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(IDPFamily_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdIDPFamilyYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempsdIDPFamilyYr1)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[7] <- specify_decimal(tempsdIDPFamilyYr1$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[8] <- specify_decimal(tempsdIDPFamilyYr1$sd[2], sigdig)

## calculate endline IDP Family year 2 SD for mean_item_var2 (7-8)
tempsdIDPFamilyYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(IDPFamily_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdIDPFamilyYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempsdIDPFamilyYr2)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[7] <- specify_decimal(tempsdIDPFamilyYr2$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[8] <- specify_decimal(tempsdIDPFamilyYr2$sd[2], sigdig)

##calculate for normality and use appropriate test to calculate significant difference in Gains between IDP Family types Year 1 (7-8)
IDPFamilyNormalityYr1 <- StatDF1 %>% 
	group_by(IDPFamily_f) %>% 
	summarise(`W Statistic` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$Statistic, 
	`p-value` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$p.value)
IDPFamilyNormalityYr1 <- as.data.frame(IDPFamilyNormalityYr1)
if (IDPFamilyNormalityYr1$`p-value`[1] > .05 && IDPFamilyNormalityYr1$`p-value`[2] > .05 )
	{	
		onewayIDPFamilyYr1 <- aov(VarItem1 ~ IDPFamily_f, data = StatDF1) 
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayIDPFamilyYr1 <- tidy(onewayIDPFamilyYr1)
		IDPFamilyPvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayIDPFamilyYr1$p.value[1]		
		} else
	{
		kruskalIDPFamilyYr1 <- kruskal.test(VarItem1 ~ IDPFamily_f, data = StatDF1)
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalIDPFamilyYr1 <- tidy(kruskalIDPFamilyYr1)
		IDPFamilyPvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalIDPFamilyYr1$p.value[1]
	}	

SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 p value`[7] <- specify_decimal(IDPFamilyPvalueYr1, sigdig5)

##calculate for normality and use appropriate test to calculate significant difference in Gains between IDP Family types Year 2 (7-8)
IDPFamilyNormalityYr2 <- StatDF2 %>% 
	group_by(IDPFamily_f) %>% 
	summarise(`W Statistic` = shapiro.test(VarItem2)$Statistic, 
	`p-value` = shapiro.test(VarItem2)$p.value)
IDPFamilyNormalityYr2 <- as.data.frame(IDPFamilyNormalityYr2)
if (IDPFamilyNormalityYr2$`p-value`[1] > .05 && IDPFamilyNormalityYr2$`p-value`[2] > .05 )
	{	
		onewayIDPFamilyYr2 <- aov(VarItem1 ~ IDPFamily_f, data = StatDF2) 
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayIDPFamilyYr2 <- tidy(onewayIDPFamilyYr2)
		IDPFamilyPvalueYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayIDPFamilyYr2$p.value[1]		
		} else
	{
		kruskalIDPFamilyYr2 <- kruskal.test(VarItem2 ~ IDPFamily_f, data = StatDF2)
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalIDPFamilyYr2 <- tidy(kruskalIDPFamilyYr2)
		IDPFamilyPvalueYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalIDPFamilyYr2$p.value[1]
	}	

SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 p value`[7] <- specify_decimal(IDPFamilyPvalueYr2, sigdig5)

## calculate Year 1 Gender based stats(9-10) for mean_item_var1
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[9] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanGenderYr1$mean[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[10] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanGenderYr1$mean[2], sigdig)

## calculate Year 2 Gender based stats(9-10) for mean_item_var2
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[9] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanGenderYr2$mean[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[10] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanGenderYr2$mean[2], sigdig)

## Calculate Year 1 Gender SD for mean_item_var1 (9-10)
tempsdGenderYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Gender_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdGenderYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempsdGenderYr1)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[9] <- specify_decimal(tempsdGenderYr1$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[10] <- specify_decimal(tempsdGenderYr1$sd[2], sigdig)

## Calculate Year 2 Gender SD for mean_item_var2 (9-10)
tempsdGenderYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Gender_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdGenderYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempsdGenderYr2)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[9] <- specify_decimal(tempsdGenderYr2$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[10] <- specify_decimal(tempsdGenderYr2$sd[2], sigdig)

## check for normality and use appropriate test to calculate significant difference in Gains between Gender types Year 1 (9-10)
GenderNormalityYr1 <- StatDF1 %>% 
	group_by(Gender_f) %>% 
	summarise(`W Statistic` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$Statistic, 
	`p-value` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$p.value)
GenderNormalityYr1 <- as.data.frame(GenderNormalityYr1)
if (GenderNormalityYr1$`p-value`[1] > .05 && GenderNormalityYr1$`p-value`[2] > .05 )
	{	
		onewayGenderYr1 <- aov(VarItem1 ~ Gender_f, data = StatDF1) 
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayGenderYr1 <- tidy(onewayGenderYr1)
		GenderPvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayGenderYr1$p.value[1]		
		} else
	{
		kruskalGenderYr1 <- kruskal.test(VarItem1 ~ Gender_f, data = StatDF1)
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalGenderYr1 <- tidy(kruskalGenderYr1)
		GenderPvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalGenderYr1$p.value[1]
	}	

SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 p value`[9] <- specify_decimal(GenderPvalueYr1, sigdig5)

SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[24] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanStateYr1$mean[3], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 Mean`[25] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanStateYr1$mean[4], sigdig)

## Calculate State Year 2 based stats(22-25)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[22] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanStateYr2$mean[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[23] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanStateYr2$mean[2], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[24] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanStateYr2$mean[3], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 Mean`[25] <- specify_decimal(tempMeanStateYr2$mean[4], sigdig)

## calculate sd state Year 1 based stats (22-25)
tempsdStateYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(State_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(BSES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdStateYr1 <- as.data.frame(tempsdStateYr1)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[22] <- specify_decimal(tempsdStateYr1$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[23] <- specify_decimal(tempsdStateYr1$sd[2], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[24] <- specify_decimal(tempsdStateYr1$sd[3], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 SD`[25] <- specify_decimal(tempsdStateYr1$sd[4], sigdig)

## calculate sd state Year 2 based stats (22-25)
tempsdStateYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(State_f) %>% summarise(sd = sd(EL2SES_Sum, na.rm = TRUE))
tempsdStateYr2 <- as.data.frame(tempsdStateYr2)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[22] <- specify_decimal(tempsdStateYr2$sd[1], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[23] <- specify_decimal(tempsdStateYr2$sd[2], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[24] <- specify_decimal(tempsdStateYr2$sd[3], sigdig)
SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 SD`[25] <- specify_decimal(tempsdStateYr2$sd[4], sigdig)

## Check for normality and use appropriate test to Calculate Significant difference in Gains between state groups for Year 1 (22-25)
StateNormalityYr1 <- StatDF1 %>% 
	group_by(State_f) %>% 
	summarise(`W Statistic` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$Statistic, 
	`p-value` = shapiro.test(VarItem1)$p.value)
StateNormalityYr1 <- as.data.frame(StateNormalityYr1)
if (StateNormalityYr1$`p-value`[1] > .05 && StateNormalityYr1$`p-value`[2] > .05  && StateNormalityYr1$`p-value`[3] > .05 && StateNormalityYr1$`p-value`[4] > .05)
	{	
		onewayStateYr1 <- aov(VarItem1 ~ State_f, data = StatDF1) 
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayStateYr1 <- tidy(onewayStateYr1)
		StatePvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayStateYr1$p.value[1]		
		} else
	{
		kruskalStateYr1 <- kruskal.test(VarItem1 ~ State_f, data = StatDF1)
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalStateYr1 <- tidy(kruskalStateYr1)
		StatePvalueYr1 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalStateYr1$p.value[1]
	}	

SESMatcheddf$`Year 1 p value`[22] <- specify_decimal(StatePvalueYr1, sigdig5)

## Check for normality and use appropriate test to Calculate Significant difference in Gains between state groups for Year 2
StateNormalityYr2 <- StatDF2 %>% 
	group_by(State_f) %>% 
	summarise(`W Statistic` = shapiro.test(VarItem2)$Statistic, 
	`p-value` = shapiro.test(VarItem2)$p.value)
StateNormalityYr2 <- as.data.frame(StateNormalityYr2)
if (StateNormalityYr2$`p-value`[1] > .05 && StateNormalityYr2$`p-value`[2] > .05  && StateNormalityYr2$`p-value`[3] > .05 && StateNormalityYr2$`p-value`[4] > .05)
	{	
		onewayStateYr2 <- aov(VarItem2 ~ State_f, data = StatDF2) 
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayStateYr2 <- tidy(onewayStateYr2)
		StatePvalueYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlieronewayStateYr2$p.value[1]		
		} else
	{
		kruskalStateYr2 <- kruskal.test(VarItem2 ~ State_f, data = StatDF2)
		EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalStateYr2 <- tidy(kruskalStateYr2)
		StatePvalueYr2 <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlierkruskalStateYr2$p.value[1]
	}	

SESMatcheddf$`Year 2 p value`[22] <- specify_decimal(StatePvalueYr2, sigdig5)


## create flextables from dataframe
set_flextable_defaults(theme_fun = theme_booktabs)
flexSESMatcheddf <- flextable(SESMatcheddf)

## set format of flextables
flexSESMatcheddf <- autofit(flexSESMatcheddf)
flexSESMatcheddf <- merge_at(flexSESMatcheddf, i=1, j=1:1, part="body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- merge_at(flexSESMatcheddf, i=12:13, j=1, part="body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- height(flexSESMatcheddf, height= .08)
flexSESMatcheddf <- width(flexSESMatcheddf, j=1, 1)
flexSESMatcheddf <- width(flexSESMatcheddf, j=2, 1)
flexSESMatcheddf <- width(flexSESMatcheddf, j=3, .7)
flexSESMatcheddf <- width(flexSESMatcheddf, j=4, .7)
flexSESMatcheddf <- width(flexSESMatcheddf, j=5, .7)
flexSESMatcheddf <- width(flexSESMatcheddf, j=6, .7)
flexSESMatcheddf <- width(flexSESMatcheddf, j=7, .7)
flexSESMatcheddf <- width(flexSESMatcheddf, j=8, .7)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=1)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=3)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=6)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=8)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=10)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=12)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=15)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=17)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=19)
flexSESMatcheddf <- hline(flexSESMatcheddf, i=21)
flexSESMatcheddf <- fontsize(flexSESMatcheddf, i = NULL, j = NULL, size = 10, part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- align(flexSESMatcheddf, i = NULL, j = 3:8, align = "right", part = "header")
flexSESMatcheddf <- align(flexSESMatcheddf, i = NULL, j = 1:2, align = "left", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- align(flexSESMatcheddf, i = NULL, j = 3:8, align = "right", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- valign(flexSESMatcheddf, i = NULL, j = NULL, part="all", valign = "top")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 2, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 4, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 6, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 8, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 10, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 12, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 14, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 16, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 18, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 20, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bg(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 22, j = 2:8, bg="#EFEFEF", part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- padding(flexSESMatcheddf, i = NULL, padding.top = 1, padding.bottom = 1)
flexSESMatcheddf <- bold(flexSESMatcheddf, i = 1, j = 1, bold = TRUE, part = "body")
flexSESMatcheddf <- bold(flexSESMatcheddf, i=1, bold = TRUE)
flexSESMatcheddf <- fix_border_issues(flexSESMatcheddf)
flexSESMatcheddf
```

#### Remaining Variances by Demographics

Student perceptions of hope, socio economic status, student overall perceptions of Safety and the influence of responsibilities of chores on school work were all analyzed for variances among the demographic subgroups.  The table below shows the p values when checking for significance of those variances. Demographic categories with only two subgroups have one line in the table representing the significance of the variance between those two subgroups and demographic categories with more than two subgroups have more than one line, with each line representing the significance of the variance between each of the subgroup groupings. A line in the table is bolded if any of the p values represent a significant variance.  In the case of more than two subgroups, the p value displayed is the adjusted p value using a Bonferroni correction.

```{r both years psychosocial All Other stats table build, include=TRUE}
dfkruskalOverallTable <- as.data.frame(matrix(nrow=22, ncol = 8))
colnames(dfkruskalOverallTable) <- c("Group", "Subgroup", "Hope Year 1 p value", "Hope Year 2 p value", "SES Year 1 p value", "SES Year 2 p value", "Overall Safety Year 2 p value", "Responsibility Influence Year 2 p value")

## Set row labels 
dfkruskalOverallTable$Group[1] <- "Program Type"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Group[2] <- "Location Type"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Group[5] <- "IDP Family"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Group[6] <- "Gender"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Group[7] <- "Age Group"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Group[8] <- "Socio-Economic Status"

dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[3] <- "IDP - Urban"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[4] <- "Rural - Urban"

dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[7] <- "4 to 11 year olds - 12 to 20 year olds"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[8] <- "high - low"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[9] <- "high - medium"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[10] <- "low - medium"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[11] <- "no - yes"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[12] <- "no - yes"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[13] <- "no - yes"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[14] <- "Maay - Maxaa tiri"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[15] <- "Maay - Other"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[16] <- "Maxaa tiri - Other"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[17] <- "Benaadir - Hirshabeelle"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[18] <- "Benaadir - Jubbaland"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[19] <- "Hirshabeelle - Jubbaland"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[20] <- "Benaadir - South-West"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[21] <- "Hirshabeelle - South-West"
dfkruskalOverallTable$Subgroup[22] <- "Jubbaland - South-West"

hope_kruskal <- build_kruskalEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MHope_Avg, EL2Hope_Avg, 'Hope Kruskal')

hope_dunn <- build_dunnEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, MHope_Avg, EL2Hope_Avg, 'Hope Dunn')

SES_kruskal <- build_kruskalEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, BSES_Sum, EL2SES_Sum, 'SES Kruskal')

SES_dunn <- build_dunnEndlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, BSES_Sum, EL2SES_Sum, 'SES Kruskal')

overallsafety_kruskal <- build_kruskalMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2Safety_Avg, 'Safety Kruskal')

overallsafety_dunn <- build_dunnMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2Safety_Avg, 'Safety Dunn')

chrinflnc_kruskal <- build_kruskalMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2CHR_INFLNC_Avg, 'Chore Influence Kruskal')

chrinflnc_dunn <- build_dunnMidlinedf_function(EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier, EL2CHR_INFLNC_Avg, 'Chore Influence Dunn')

dfkruskalOverallTable[1,3] <- hope_kruskal[1,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[1,4] <- hope_kruskal[1,5]
dfkruskalOverallTable[1,5] <- SES_kruskal[1,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[1,6] <- SES_kruskal[1,5]
dfkruskalOverallTable[1,7] <- overallsafety_kruskal[1,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[1,8] <- chrinflnc_kruskal[1,3]

dfkruskalOverallTable[5,8] <- chrinflnc_kruskal[3,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[6,3] <- hope_kruskal[4,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[6,4] <- hope_kruskal[4,5]

dfkruskalOverallTable[7,3] <- hope_kruskal[5,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[7,4] <- hope_kruskal[5,5]
dfkruskalOverallTable[7,5] <- SES_kruskal[5,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[7,6] <- SES_kruskal[5,5]
dfkruskalOverallTable[7,7] <- overallsafety_kruskal[5,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[7,8] <- chrinflnc_kruskal[5,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[11,3] <- hope_kruskal[7,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[11,4] <- hope_kruskal[7,5]
dfkruskalOverallTable[11,5] <- SES_kruskal[7,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[11,6] <- SES_kruskal[7,5]
dfkruskalOverallTable[11,7] <- overallsafety_kruskal[7,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[11,8] <- chrinflnc_kruskal[7,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[12,3] <- hope_kruskal[8,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[12,4] <- hope_kruskal[8,5]
dfkruskalOverallTable[12,5] <- SES_kruskal[8,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[12,6] <- SES_kruskal[8,5]
dfkruskalOverallTable[12,7] <- overallsafety_kruskal[8,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[12,8] <- chrinflnc_kruskal[8,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[13,3] <- hope_kruskal[9,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[13,4] <- hope_kruskal[9,5]
dfkruskalOverallTable[13,5] <- SES_kruskal[9,3]

dfkruskalOverallTable[13,7] <- overallsafety_kruskal[9,3]
dfkruskalOverallTable[13,8] <- chrinflnc_kruskal[9,3]

dfkruskalOverallTable[2:4,3] <- hope_dunn[1:3,4]
dfkruskalOverallTable[2:4,4] <- hope_dunn[1:3,6]
dfkruskalOverallTable[2:4,5] <- SES_dunn[1:3,4]
dfkruskalOverallTable[2:4,6] <- SES_dunn[1:3,6]
dfkruskalOverallTable[2:4,7] <- overallsafety_dunn[1:3,4]
dfkruskalOverallTable[2:4,8] <- chrinflnc_dunn[1:3,4]

dfkruskalOverallTable[8:10,4] <- hope_dunn[4:6,6]
dfkruskalOverallTable[8:10,5] <- SES_dunn[4:6,4]

dfkruskalOverallTable[8:10,7] <- overallsafety_dunn[4:6,4]
dfkruskalOverallTable[8:10,8] <- chrinflnc_dunn[1:3,4]

dfkruskalOverallTable[14:16,4] <- hope_dunn[7:9,6]
dfkruskalOverallTable[14:16,5] <- SES_dunn[7:9,4]

dfkruskalOverallTable[14:16,7] <- overallsafety_dunn[7:9,4]
dfkruskalOverallTable[14:16,8] <- chrinflnc_dunn[7:9,4]
dfkruskalOverallTable[17:22,3] <- hope_dunn[10:15,4]
dfkruskalOverallTable[17:22,4] <- hope_dunn[10:15,6]
dfkruskalOverallTable[17:22,5] <- SES_dunn[10:15,4]
dfkruskalOverallTable[17:22,6] <- SES_dunn[10:15,6]
dfkruskalOverallTable[17:22,7] <- overallsafety_dunn[10:15,4]
dfkruskalOverallTable[17:22,8] <- chrinflnc_dunn[10:15,4]

## create flextables from dataframe
set_flextable_defaults(theme_fun = theme_booktabs)
flexdfkruskalOverallTable <- flextable(dfkruskalOverallTable)

## set format of flextable
flexdfkruskalOverallTable <- autofit(flexdfkruskalOverallTable)
flexdfkruskalOverallTable <- height(flexdfkruskalOverallTable, height=0.08)
flexdfkruskalOverallTable <- width(flexdfkruskalOverallTable, j=1, 1)
flexdfkruskalOverallTable <- width(flexdfkruskalOverallTable, j=2, 2)
flexdfkruskalOverallTable <- width(flexdfkruskalOverallTable, j=3, .7)
```
