#### Head Teacher Teaching Experience

Head teachers were asked if they had ever taught in the classroom.

<br/>

```{r head teacher teaching experience, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=4.0}

HeadTeachExp <- BMEL2headteachersurveylonglocate %>% group_by(HTTaughtClassroom) %>% count()

HeadTeachExp <- as.data.frame(HeadTeachExp)

## replace na's with 0
##HeadTeachExp <- drop_na(HeadTeachExp)
HeadTeachExp[is.na(HeadTeachExp)] = 0

HeadTeachExp <- HeadTeachExp %>% arrange(desc(n))

## #1C9099 - Teal, #D0D1E6 - lilac

## set square color for waffle chart
HeadTeachExp <- HeadTeachExp %>% mutate(squareColor = ifelse(HTTaughtClassroom==0, "#D0D1E6", "#1C9099"))

## create new labels for whether or not head teacher teaches
HeadTeachExp <- HeadTeachExp %>% mutate(Teaches = ifelse(HTTaughtClassroom==0, "No", "Yes"))

## create percent column
HeadTeachExp <- HeadTeachExp %>% mutate(percent = round((n/BMEL2NumHeadTeachers)*100, 0))

## create percentType square color
HeadTeachExp <- HeadTeachExp %>% mutate(percentType = ifelse(HTTaughtClassroom==0, "background", "fillColor"))
                                        
hdt_percent_filled <- HeadTeachExp[HeadTeachExp$percentType == "fillColor",]$percent
hdt_percent_filled <- paste0(hdt_percent_filled, "%")

## create object placeholder for waffle name
hdt_name <- "of Head Teachers have taught in the classroom"

## add chart title
# first layer: identify the data source and set aesthetics
ggplot(data = HeadTeachExp, aes(fill = squareColor, values = percent)) +
  # second layer: define waffle chart dimensions, color, and border thickness
  geom_waffle(n_cols = 10, size = 0.5, colour = "#FFFFFF", 
              flip = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE) +
  # third layer: specify square colors
    scale_fill_manual(values = HeadTeachExp$squareColor) +
  # add a title using HTML
  labs(title = paste0("<span style='color:#1C9099;'>", hdt_percent_filled ," </span>",
    "<span style='color:#262626'>", hdt_name ,"</span>")) +
  theme_void() +
  # update theme: add element_markdown to apply custom HTML formatting
  theme(plot.title = element_markdown(family = "Helvetica", face = "bold",
                                      hjust = 0.15, vjust = 0.5, size = 20),
        # add margins to plot
        plot.margin = margin(t = 0.5, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0, "cm"))
```

<br/>

For the Head Teachers that report having taught in a classroom they were then asked what grade or type of class they have experience teaching.

<br/>

```{r head teacher teaching experiences list, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5,  fig.height=4.0}

HeadTchrExp <- BMEL2headteachersurveylonglocate %>% dplyr::select("Qur'anic school"=HTTaught_01, "Grade 1"=HTTaught_02, "Grade 2"=HTTaught_03, "Grade 3"=HTTaught_04, "Grade 4"=HTTaught_05, "Grade 5"=HTTaught_06, "Above Grade 5"=HTTaught_07)

HeadTchrExp <- as.data.frame(HeadTchrExp)
HeadTchrExp <- HeadTchrExp %>% mutate(Base='Head Teachers')
HeadTchrExp <- drop_na(HeadTchrExp)

HeadTchrExpAggregate <- aggregate(.~Base, data=HeadTchrExp, FUN=sum)
HeadTchrExppivot1 <- HeadTchrExpAggregate %>% pivot_longer(!Base, names_to = 'Level', values_to = 'Response')
HeadTchrExppivot1 <- as.data.frame(HeadTchrExppivot1)
HeadTchrExppivot1$Level <- sapply(HeadTchrExppivot1$Level, as.factor)
HeadTchrExppivot1$Level <- fct_reorder(HeadTchrExppivot1$Level, HeadTchrExppivot1$Response)
HeadTchrExpOverall <- HeadTchrExppivot1[!(is.na(HeadTchrExppivot1$Response) | HeadTchrExppivot1$Response=="0"),]

HeadTchrExpOverall$Level <- fct_relevel(HeadTchrExpOverall$Level, "Above Grade 5", "Grade 5", "Grade 4", "Grade 3", "Grade 2", "Grade 1", "Qur'anic school")

HeadTchrExpOverall <- as.data.frame(HeadTchrExpOverall)
HeadTchrExpOverall$Base <- sapply(HeadTchrExpOverall$Base, as.factor)
HeadTchrExpOverall$Base <- fct_relevel(HeadTchrExpOverall$Base, "Head Teachers")

HeadTchrExpOverall <- HeadTchrExpOverall %>% mutate(impact = round( Response/BMEL2NumHeadTeachers, 2))

HeadTchrExpOverall <- HeadTchrExpOverall %>% mutate(percentage = paste0(impact*100, "%"))

## create impact color for category with highest percentage
HeadTchrExpOverall <- HeadTchrExpOverall %>% mutate(barcolor = ifelse(Level == "Above Grade 5", "#1C9099", "grey"))

hdt_level_title1 = "Head Teachers have more experience teaching"
hdt_level_title2 = "\nthan any other grade level"

hdt_level_titleMax = "Above Grade 5"

g.plot <- ggplot(HeadTchrExpOverall) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", 
    position = "identity", 
    fill = HeadTchrExpOverall$barcolor,
    width = 0.75,
    aes(x = reorder(Level, impact), y = impact), 
    ) + scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + 
  coord_flip() + theme_minimal() + theme(axis.title = element_blank(), 
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10, face = "bold", color = "gray40"),
        axis.text.x = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid = element_blank(), 
        plot.margin = unit(c(1.3, 1, 0, 1), "lines")) + annotate(geom = "text",
    label = HeadTchrExpOverall$percentage, 
    x = HeadTchrExpOverall$Level, 
    y = HeadTchrExpOverall$impact - 0.015,
    color = "white",
    family = "sans",
    fontface = "bold",
    size = 4,
    hjust = 1
    ) + # add a title using HTML
  labs(title = paste0("<span style='color:#262626'>", hdt_level_title1, " </span>", " <BR/>", "<span style='color:#1C9099;'>", hdt_level_titleMax," </span>", 
    "<span style='color:#262626'>", hdt_level_title2 ,"</span>")) +
  # update theme: add element_markdown to apply custom HTML formatting
  theme(plot.title = element_markdown(family = "Helvetica", face = "bold",
                                      hjust = 0.15, vjust = 0.5, size = 16))
        
print(g.plot)

```




### Teacher and Head Teacher Perceptions of Safety

At Endline Teachers and Head Teachers were asked about their overall safety.  The scale items were used to assess general teacher safety in all three areas they spend time in. The scale is composed of the average of the following 3 items: 

<br/>

 * I feel safe at home.
 * I feel safe at school.
 * I feel safe in my community.
 
<br/>

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-strongly disagree, 1-disagree, 2-somewhat disagree, 3-neither agree nor disagree, 4-somewhat agree, 5-agree, 6-strongly agree) where teachers indicated their perceived safety of themselves. The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison between teachers and head teachers.  They values are then shown in table format disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. This is followed by a vsiual representation of the data in horizontal stacked bar graphs otherwise known as likert graphs.

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher safety, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_agreedisagree_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TSafetyCNV_LKRT, EL2HTSafetyCNV_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher and Head Teacher Overall Safety Scale')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher overall safety table build, include=TRUE}
EL2TeacherSafetyTable <- build_median_teacher_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2TSafetyScaleCNV, "Overall Safety")
```

`r EL2TeacherSafetyTable`

<br/>

```{r head teacher overall safety table build, include=TRUE}
EL2HTeacherSafetyTable <- build_median_headteacher_function(EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2HTSafetyScaleCNV, "Overall Safety")
```

`r EL2HTeacherSafetyTable`

<br/>

```{r teacher overall safety likert, include=FALSE, fig.height=6, fig.width=6.5}
build_teacherlikert_agreedisagree_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2TSafetyCNV_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher Safety Scale', -100, 100, 20)
```

<br/>

```{r head teacher overall safety likert, include=FALSE, fig.height=6, fig.width=6.5}
build_headteacherlikert_agreedisagree_function(EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2HTSafetyCNV_LKRT, 'Endline Head Teacher Safety Scale', -100, 100, 20)
```

<br/>

### Teacher's ability to teach when feeling unsafe

Teachers were given 6 items to rate how frequently feeling unsafe at home, at school, and in the community each influence their ability to complete 6 tasks related to teaching, as follows:

<br/>

* If I feel unsafe at home/school/in the community, it is difficult to teach at school.
* If I feel unsafe at home/school/in the community, it is difficult to attend school.
* If I feel unsafe at home/school/in the community, it is difficult to focus on teaching at school.
* If I feel unsafe at home/school/in the community, it is difficult to do my tasks at school.
* If I feel unsafe at home/school/in the community, it is difficult to care about teaching.
* If I feel unsafe at home/school/in the community, it is difficult to like teaching.

<br/>

All items were collected on a 6 point Likert scale (0-none of the time, 1-a little of the time, 2-some of the time, 3-a lot of the time, 4-most of the time, 5- all of the time) where teachers in. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison of home/school/in the community. That is followed by a table disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. This is followed by a vsiual representation of the data in likert graphs. Head teachers were not given these items to rate and are not therefore not shown.

```{r teacher safey influence likert graph, include=TRUE}

	## set column order
	col_order <- c("Item", "none of the time", "a little of the time", "some of the time", "a lot of the time", "most of the time", "all of the time")

	## get teacher nrows SafetyHome
	NumTeachersHome <- nrow(EL2teachersurveylonglocate %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TSafetyHome_LKRT)))
	
	## get teacher frequency percents for safety home
	tempteacherHome <- EL2teachersurveylonglocate %>% dplyr::select(EL2TSafetyHome_LKRT) %>% group_by(EL2TSafetyHome_LKRT) %>% count()
	tempteacherHome <- as.data.frame(tempteacherHome)
	tempteacherHome <- mutate_if(tempteacherHome, is.factor, as.character)
	
	tempteacherHome <- tempteacherHome %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TSafetyHome_LKRT))
	tempteacherHome <- tempteacherHome %>% group_by() %>% mutate(per = round((n/NumTeachersHome * 100), 0))
	tempteacherHome$n <- NULL
	
	teacherlengthHome <- nrow(tempteacherHome)
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+1,1] <- 'none of the time'
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+1,2] <- 0
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+2,1] <- 'a little of the time'
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+2,2] <- 0
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+3,1] <- 'some of the time'
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+3,2] <- 0
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+4,1] <- 'a lot of the time'
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+4,2] <- 0
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+5,1] <- 'most of the time'
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+5,2] <- 0
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+6,1] <- 'all of the time'
	tempteacherHome[teacherlengthHome+6,2] <- 0
	
	## pivot values from rows to columns, while adding the values from per column to integrate 0 values
	tempteacherHomePivot <- tempteacherHome %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TSafetyHome_LKRT, values_from = per, values_fn = sum)
	tempteacherHomePivot <- as.data.frame(tempteacherHomePivot)
	tempteacherHomePivot[is.na(tempteacherHomePivot)] <- 0
	tempteacherHomePivot <- tempteacherHomePivot %>% mutate(Item="Unsafe at Home")
	tempteacherHomePivot <- tempteacherHomePivot[, col_order]
	
	
	## get teacher nrows SafetySchool
	NumTeachersSchool <- nrow(EL2teachersurveylonglocate %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TSafetySchool_LKRT)))
	
	## get teacher frequency percents for safety home
	tempteacherSchool <- EL2teachersurveylonglocate %>% dplyr::select(EL2TSafetySchool_LKRT) %>% group_by(EL2TSafetySchool_LKRT) %>% count()
	tempteacherSchool <- as.data.frame(tempteacherSchool)
	tempteacherSchool <- mutate_if(tempteacherSchool, is.factor, as.character)
	
	tempteacherSchool <- tempteacherSchool %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TSafetySchool_LKRT))
	tempteacherSchool <- tempteacherSchool %>% group_by() %>% mutate(per = round((n/NumTeachersSchool * 100), 0))
	tempteacherSchool$n <- NULL
	
	teacherlengthSchool <- nrow(tempteacherSchool)
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+1,1] <- 'none of the time'
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+1,2] <- 0
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+2,1] <- 'a little of the time'
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+2,2] <- 0
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+3,1] <- 'some of the time'
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+3,2] <- 0
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+4,1] <- 'a lot of the time'
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+4,2] <- 0
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+5,1] <- 'most of the time'
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+5,2] <- 0
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+6,1] <- 'all of the time'
	tempteacherSchool[teacherlengthSchool+6,2] <- 0
	
	## pivot values from rows to columns, while adding the values from per column to integrate 0 values
	tempteacherSchoolPivot <- tempteacherSchool %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TSafetySchool_LKRT, values_from = per, values_fn = sum)
	tempteacherSchoolPivot <- as.data.frame(tempteacherSchoolPivot)
	tempteacherSchoolPivot[is.na(tempteacherSchoolPivot)] <- 0
	tempteacherSchoolPivot <- tempteacherSchoolPivot %>% mutate(Item="Unsafe at School")
	tempteacherSchoolPivot <- tempteacherSchoolPivot[, col_order]
	
	
	## get teacher nrows SafetyCmmnty
	NumTeachersCmmnty <- nrow(EL2teachersurveylonglocate %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TSafetyCmmnty_LKRT)))
	
	## get teacher frequency percents for safety home
	tempteacherCmmnty <- EL2teachersurveylonglocate %>% dplyr::select(EL2TSafetyCmmnty_LKRT) %>% group_by(EL2TSafetyCmmnty_LKRT) %>% count()
	tempteacherCmmnty <- as.data.frame(tempteacherCmmnty)
	tempteacherCmmnty <- mutate_if(tempteacherCmmnty, is.factor, as.character)
	
	tempteacherCmmnty <- tempteacherCmmnty %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TSafetyCmmnty_LKRT))
	tempteacherCmmnty <- tempteacherCmmnty %>% group_by() %>% mutate(per = round((n/NumTeachersCmmnty * 100), 0))
	tempteacherCmmnty$n <- NULL
	
	teacherlengthCmmnty <- nrow(tempteacherCmmnty)
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+1,1] <- 'none of the time'
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+1,2] <- 0
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+2,1] <- 'a little of the time'
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+2,2] <- 0
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+3,1] <- 'some of the time'
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+3,2] <- 0
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+4,1] <- 'a lot of the time'
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+4,2] <- 0
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+5,1] <- 'most of the time'
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+5,2] <- 0
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+6,1] <- 'all of the time'
	tempteacherCmmnty[teacherlengthCmmnty+6,2] <- 0
	
	## pivot values from rows to columns, while adding the values from per column to integrate 0 values
	tempteacherCmmntyPivot <- tempteacherCmmnty %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TSafetyCmmnty_LKRT, values_from = per, values_fn = sum)
	tempteacherCmmntyPivot <- as.data.frame(tempteacherCmmntyPivot)
	tempteacherCmmntyPivot[is.na(tempteacherCmmntyPivot)] <- 0
	tempteacherCmmntyPivot <- tempteacherCmmntyPivot %>% mutate(Item="Unsafe in the Community")
	tempteacherCmmntyPivot <- tempteacherCmmntyPivot[, col_order]
	
	
	## combine teacher and head teacher pivots
	tempteacherPivotTotal <- rbind(tempteacherCmmntyPivot, tempteacherSchoolPivot, tempteacherHomePivot)
	tempteacherPivotTotal <- as.data.frame(tempteacherPivotTotal)
	tempteacherPivotTotal$Item <- sapply(tempteacherPivotTotal$Item, as.factor)
	tempteacherPivotTotal <- tempteacherPivotTotal %>% arrange(Item)
	
	origNames = colnames(tempteacherPivotTotal)
	
	# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "1%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "2%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "3%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "4%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "5%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "6%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "7%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "8%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "9%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
	tempteacherUnsafePlot <- plot.likert(Item ~ ., tempteacherPivotTotal,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=3.5,
            xlim=c(-100,100), scales=list(x=list(at=seq(-100,100,20)), y=list(relation="free")),
            main = list(label='How frequently does a feeling of unsafety\naffect Teaching abilities?', cex=1), col = likertPalette2,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = T,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            ## set size of the legend text and the number of columns
            auto.key.in = list(cex = .7, columns=4),
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)))

tempteacherUnsafePlot
	
	

```

### Teacher and Head Teacher Perceptions of their own Hope

Teachers and Head Teachers were given 6 items to rate the perception of their own hope, as follows:

<br/>

* I think I am doing pretty well.
* I can think of many ways to get the things in life that are most important to me.
* I am doing at least as well as other people in my community.
* When I have a problem, I can come up with many ways to solve it.
* I think the things I've done in the past will help me in the future.
* Even when others want to quit, I know that I can find ways to solve the problem.

<br/>

All items were collected on a 6 point Likert scale (0-none of the time, 1-a little of the time, 2-some of the time, 3-a lot of the time, 4-most of the time, 5- all of the time). The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison of teachers and head teachers. That is followed by a table disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. 


<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher hope, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_nonealltime_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2THope_LKRT, EL2HTHope_LKRT, 'How often do Teachers and Head Teachers feel hopeful?')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher hope table build, include=TRUE}
EL2TeacherHopeTable <- build_median_teacher_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2THopeScale, "Perception of Hope")
```

`r EL2TeacherHopeTable`

<br/>

```{r head teacher hope table build, include=TRUE}
EL2HTeacherHopeTable <- build_median_headteacher_function(EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2HTHopeScale, "Perception of Hope")
```

`r EL2HTeacherHopeTable`

<br/>

### Teacher and Head Teacher Perceptions of their Students' Hope

Similar to the perception of their own hope, Teachers and Head Teachers were given 6 items to rate the perception of the hope of their students, as follows:

<br/>

* I think my students are doing pretty well.
* My students can think of many ways to get the things in life that are most important to them.
* My students are doing just as well as other kids their age.
* When my students have a problem, they can come up with many ways to solve it.
* I think the things my students have done in the past will help them in the future.
* Even when others want to quit, I know that my students can find ways to solve the problem.

<br/>

All items were collected on a 6 point Likert scale (0-none of the time, 1-a little of the time, 2-some of the time, 3-a lot of the time, 4-most of the time, 5- all of the time). The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison of teachers and head teachers. That is followed by a table disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. 

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher student hope, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_nonealltime_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TStudentHope_LKRT, EL2HTStudentHope_LKRT, 'How often do Students feel Hopeful as perceived by Teachers and Head Teachers?')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher student hope table build, include=TRUE}
EL2TeacherStudentHopeTable <- build_median_teacher_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2TStudentHopeScale, "Perception of Students' Hope")
```

`r EL2TeacherStudentHopeTable`

<br/>

```{r head teacher student hope table build, include=TRUE}

```

### Teacher and Head Teacher Perceptions of Equity in the Classroom

<br/>

```{r teacher and head teacher equity alpha, include=FALSE}

equity_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Equity Scale Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

equity_alpha_teacher <- round(equity_alpha_teacher, 2)

```

Teachers and Head Teachers were both asked a set of 3 items to identify their perceptions of student equity in the classroom at endline as follows:

<br/>

* I treat students fairly at school.
* In my school, all children are treated the same.
* In my school, all children are treated fairly.

<br/>

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-Very Untrue, 1-Untrue, 2-Somewhat Untrue, 3-Neutral, 4-Somewhat True, 5-True, 6-Very True) where teachers indicated their perceived equity in the classroom at Endline. The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison of teachers and head teachers. That is followed by a table disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. 

<br/>

The Teacher Equity Scale was test for reliability with a cronbach aplha of: `r equity_alpha_teacher` showing a high reliability.

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher equity, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_untruetrue_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TEquity_LKRT, EL2HTEquity_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher and Head Teacher Perception of Equity in the Classroom')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher equity table build, include=TRUE}
EL2TeacherEquityTable <- build_median_teacher_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2TEquityScaleCNV, "Perception of Equity")
```

`r EL2TeacherEquityTable`

<br/>

```{r head teacher equity table build, include=TRUE}
EL2HTeacherEquityTable <- build_median_headteacher_function(EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2HTEquityScaleCNV, "Perception of Equity")
```

`r EL2HTeacherEquityTable`

### Teacher and Head Teacher Perceptions of Internal Support in the Classroom

<br/>

```{r teacher and head teacher internal support alpha, include=FALSE}

internal_support_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Internal Support Scale Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

internal_support_alpha_teacher <- round(internal_support_alpha_teacher, 2)
```

The Internal Support scale items were used to assess Teacher’s perception of internal support in the school environment. When tested for reliability the Internal dimension showed a high level of reliability with a cronbach alpha of: `r  internal_support_alpha_teacher`. With the high level of reliability the scale is shown below as a scale. 

<br/>

Internal Dimension:

<br/>

*	I have the content knowledge I need to effectively teach my class.
*	I have a range of techniques to effectively teach all students in my class.
*	I have the knowledge and skills I need to effectively teach all children in my class, regardless of their gender, age, or family background, disability, or other characteristics.
*	I have various strategies to effectively manage my classroom.

<br/>

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-Very Untrue, 1-Untrue, 2-Somewhat Untrue, 3-Neutral, 4-Somewhat True, 5-True, 6-Very True) where teachers indicated their perceived internal support in the classroom at Endline. The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison of teachers and head teachers. The likert figure is followed by a table of teacher internal support medians for midline and endline disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. The final table contains the head teacher internal support medians for endline only because this data was only collected at endline. 

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher internal support, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_untruetrue_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TSupportInt_LKRT, EL2HTSupportInt_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher and Head Teacher Perception of Internal Support in the Classroom')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher internal support table build, include=TRUE}
TeacherIntSupportTable <- build_median_ME_teacher_function(BMEL2teachersurveylonglocate, MTSupportIntDimCNV, EL2TSupportIntDimCNV, "Perception of Internal Support")
```

`r TeacherIntSupportTable`

<br/>

```{r head teacher internal support table build, include=TRUE}
EL2HTeacherIntSupportTable <- build_median_headteacher_function(EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2HTSupportIntDimCNV, "Perception of Internal Support")
```

`r EL2HTeacherIntSupportTable`

<br/>

### Teacher and Head Teacher Perceptions of External Support in the Classroom

<br/>

```{r teacher and head teacher External support alpha, include=FALSE}

external_support_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher External Support Scale Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

external_support_alpha_teacher <- round(external_support_alpha_teacher, 2)

```


The External Support scale items were used to assess Teacher’s perception of external support in the school environment. The External dimension showed a low level of reliability with a cronbach alpha of `r external_support_alpha_teacher`.  With the low level of reliability, the external items are shown as individual items instead of a complete scale.

<br/>

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-Very Untrue, 1-Untrue, 2-Somewhat Untrue, 3-Neutral, 4-Somewhat True, 5-True, 6-Very True) where teachers indicated their perceived internal support in the classroom at Endline. The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison of teachers and head teachers. The likert figure is followed by a table of teacher external support medians for midline and endline disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. The final table contains the head teacher external support medians for endline only because this data was only collected at endline. 

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher ext support 03, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_untruetrue_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TSupport_03CNV_f, EL2HTSupport_03CNV_f, 'Endline: I have the support I need\nto effectively teach my class/run my school')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher ext support 03 table build, include=TRUE}
EL2TeacherSupport03Table <- build_median_ME_teacher_function(BMEL2teachersurveylonglocate, MTSupport_03CNV_n, EL2TSupport_03CNV_n, "Have the support they need to effectively teach their class")
```

`r EL2TeacherSupport03Table`

### Teacher Self-Efficacy for Instruction

<br/>

```{r teacher and head teacher instrSE alpha, include=FALSE}

instrse_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Instructional Self-Efficacy Scale Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

instrse_alpha_teacher <- round(instrse_alpha_teacher, 2)

```

Teachers were given a set of 11 questions to identify their instructional self-efficacy in the classroom at endline.  Head Teachers, although sometimes filling the role of a teacher, were not presented with these questions.  The Teacher Instructional Self-Efficacy Scale was test for reliability with a cronbach aplha of: `r instrse_alpha_teacher` showing a high reliability. The 11 questions were as follows:

<br/>

* How much can you do to influence the class sizes in your school?  
* How much can you do to get through to the most difficult students?  
* How much can you do to promote learning when there is lack of support from the home?
* How much can you do to keep students on task on difficult assignments? 
* How much can you do to increase students’ memory of what they have been taught in previous lessons?
* How much can you do to motivate students who show low interest in schoolwork?
* How much can you do to get students to work together?
* How much can you do to overcome the influence of adverse community conditions on students’ learning?
* How much can you do to get children to do their homework?  
* How much can you do to help students focus on and get through difficult assignments? 

<br/>

All items were collected on a 5 point Likert scale (0-Nothing, 1-Very little, 2-Some influence, 3-Quite a bit, 4-A great deal) where teachers indicated their instructional self-efficacy in the classroom at the end of year 1 and the end of year 2. The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the range of teacher responses. That is followed by a table disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. 

<br/>


```{r call simple likert for teacher instructional self-efficacy, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherlikert_nothingagreatdeal_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2TInstrSE_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher Instructor Self-Efficacy')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher instructor self-efficacy table build, include=TRUE}
EL2TeacherInstrSETable <- build_median_ME_teacher_function(BMEL2teachersurveylonglocate, MTInstrSEScaleCNV, EL2TInstrSEScaleCNV, "Instructor Self-Efficacy")
```

`r EL2TeacherInstrSETable`

### Teacher and Head Teacher Emotional Engagement

<br/>

```{r teacher and head teacher emotional engagement alpha, include=FALSE}

emotionalengagement_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Emotional Engagement Dim Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

emotionalengagement_alpha_teacher <- round(emotionalengagement_alpha_teacher, 2)

```

Teachers were given a set of 5 items to identify their emotional engagement with teaching in the classroom at the end of year 1 and the end of year 2.  Head Teachers, although sometimes filling the role of a teacher, were not presented with these items.  When tested for reliability the emotional engagement dimension at endline, the dimension showed a high level of reliability with a cronbach alpha of: `r  emotionalengagement_alpha_teacher`. With the high level of reliability the scale is shown below as a scale. 

<br/>

Emotional Engagement Dimension:

<br/>

* I am excited about teaching.
* I feel happy while teaching.
* I love teaching.
* I find teaching enjoyable.
* I enjoy teaching.

<br/>

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-Never, 1-Rarely, 2-On occasion, 3-Sometimes, 4-Often, 5-Frequently, 6-Always) where teachers indicated their emotional engagement in the classroom at the end of year 1 and the end of year 2. The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the range of teacher responses for the overall emotional engagement dimension. The likert figure is followed by a table of teacher emotional engagement dimension medians for midline and endline disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. 

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher emotional engagement dimension, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}

```

```{r teacher and head teacher social engagement alpha, include=FALSE}
socialengagement_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Social Engagement Dim Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

socialengagement_alpha_teacher <- round(socialengagement_alpha_teacher, 2)

```

Teachers were given a set of 5 items to identify their social engagement with teaching in the classroom at the end of year 1 and the end of year 2.  Head Teachers, although sometimes filling the role of a teacher, were not presented with these items.  When tested for reliability the social engagement dimension at endline, the dimension showed a high level of reliability with a cronbach alpha of: `r  socialengagement_alpha_teacher`. With the high level of reliability the scale is shown below as a scale. 

<br/>

Social Engagement Dimension:

<br/>

* In class, I show warmth to my students.
* In class, I am aware of my students’ feelings.
* In class, I care about the problems of my students.
* In class, I am empathetic toward my students.
* In class, I am aware of the way my students feel.


<br/>

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-Never, 1-Rarely, 2-On occasion, 3-Sometimes, 4-Often, 5-Frequently, 6-Always) where teachers indicated their social engagement in the classroom at the end of year 1 and the end of year 2. The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the range of teacher responses for the overall social engagement dimension. The likert figure is followed by a table of teacher social engagement dimension medians for midline and endline disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state.

### Teacher and Head Teacher Satisfaction

```{r teacher and head teacher satisfaction alpha, include=FALSE}

satisfaction_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Satisfaction NTPS Scale Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

satisfaction_alpha_teacher <- round(satisfaction_alpha_teacher, 2)
```

<br/>

Teachers were given a set of 7 items to identify their satisfaction with teaching in the classroom at the end of year 1 and again at the end of year 2. One of the items was worded in the negative sense and did not perform as expected, so it was removed from the scale. The remaining 6 items were combined as a scale and tested for reliability at the end of year 2. The scale showed a high level of reliability with a cronbach alpha of: `r  satisfaction_alpha_teacher`. With the high level of reliability the scale is shown below as a scale. The 6 items of the satisfaction scale was as follows:

<br/>

  * The head teacher's behavior toward the staff is supportive and encouraging.
  * My head teacher enforces school rules for student conduct and backs me up when I need it.
  * I receive a great deal of support from parents for the work I do.
  * I am generally satisfied with being a teacher at this school.
  * There is a great deal of cooperative effort among the staff members.
  * The teachers at this school like being here; I would describe us as a satisfied group.

<br/>

Head Teachers were given 4 items at the end of year 1 and 5 items at the end of year 2. The 5 items at the end of year 2 were used for the head teacher scale and overlap greatly with the teacher satisfaction scale as follows:

<br/>

  * I get the support and training I need to do my job effectively.
  * I receive a great deal of support from parents for the work I do.
  * I am generally satisfied with being Head Teacher at this school.
  * There is a great deal of cooperative effort among the staff members.
  * Any stress involved in serving as head teacher at this school is worth it

<br/>

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-strongly disagree, 1-disagree, 2-somewhat disagree, 3-neither agree nor disagree, 4-somewhat agree, 5-agree, 6-strongly agree). The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison between teachers and head teachers.  They values are then shown in table format disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state first for teachers and then for head teachers.
  
```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher satisfaction, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_agreedisagree_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TSatisNTPS_LKRT, EL2HTSatis_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher and Head Teacher Satisfaction')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher satisfaction table build, include=TRUE}
EL2TeacherSatisfactionTable <- build_median_teacher_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2TSatisNTPSScaleCNV, "Satisfaction")
```

`r EL2TeacherSatisfactionTable`

<br/>

```{r head teacher satisfaction table build, include=TRUE}
EL2HTeacherSatisfactionTable <- build_median_headteacher_function(EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2HTSatisScaleCNV, "Satisfaction")
```

`r EL2HTeacherSatisfactionTable`

  

### Teacher and Head Teacher Training

```{r teacher and head teacher training alpha, include=FALSE}

training_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Training Scale Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

training_alpha_teacher <- round(training_alpha_teacher, 2)

```


At the end of year 2 Teachers and Head Teachers were asked their level of agreement to 7 statements about the BAB training they received. The 7 statements were combined to form a scale and test for reliability with a cronbach aplha of: `r training_alpha_teacher` showing a high reliability. The scale was composed as the mean of the following 7 items: 

<br/>

* The BAB program training I received gave me the knowledge and skills I needed to effectively teach the curriculum/run my school.
* The BAB program training I received improved student learning opportunities in the classroom/in my school.
* The BAB program training I received encouraged me to reflect on the way I teach/run my school.
* The BAB program training I received gave me the knowledge and skills I need to effectively teach all children in my classrooms/in my school.
* The BAB program training gave me the knowledge and skills I need to teach/support teaching children at an accelerated pace.
* The BAB program training gave me the knowledge and skills I need to teach/support children how to participate in community activities.
* The BAB program training I received encouraged me to engage members of the community in what my class/school is doing.

<br/>

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-strongly disagree, 1-disagree, 2-somewhat disagree, 3-neither agree nor disagree, 4-somewhat agree, 5-agree, 6-strongly agree). The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison between teachers and head teachers.  They values are then shown in table format disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. 

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher BAB training, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_agreedisagree_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TTrainingCNV_LKRT, EL2HTTrainingCNV_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher and Head Teacher BAB Training')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher BAB training table build, include=TRUE}
EL2TeacherTrainingTable <- build_median_teacher_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2TTrainingScaleCNV, "BAB Training")
```

`r EL2TeacherTrainingTable`

<br/>

```{r head teacher BAB training table build, include=TRUE}

```

`r EL2HTeacherTrainingTable`

Cognitive Engagement Dimension:

<br/>

* I try my hardest to perform well while teaching.
* While teaching, I really –”throw” myself into my work.
* While teaching I pay a lot of attention to my work.

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-Never, 1-Rarely, 2-On occasion, 3-Sometimes, 4-Often, 5-Frequently, 6-Always) where teachers indicated their cognitive engagement in the classroom at the end of year 1 and the end of year 2. The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the range of teacher responses for the overall cognitive engagement dimension. ### Teacher and Head Teacher Efficacy to Create a Positive School Climate

```{r teacher and head teacher posschlclimate alpha, include=FALSE}

posschlclimate_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Positive School Climate Scale Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

posschlclimate_alpha_teacher <- round(posschlclimate_alpha_teacher, 2)

```


Teachers and Head teachers were given a set of 8 questions to identify their efficacy to create a positive school climate.

<br/>

* How much can you do to make the school a safe place?
* How much can you do to help students enjoy coming to school?
* How much can you do to help students trust teachers?
* How much can you help your teachers with their teaching skills?
* How much can you do to enhance collaboration between teachers and the administration to make the school run effectively?
* How much can you do to reduce school dropout?
* How much can you do to reduce school absenteeism?
* How much can you do to get students to believe they can do well in schoolwork? 

<br/>

All items were collected on a 5 point Likert scale (0-Nothing, 1-Very little, 2-Some influence, 3-Quite a bit, 4-A great deal) where teachers indicated their efficacy to build a positive school climate in the classroom at midline and endline. Head teachers were given the same items to measure their efficacy to build a positive school climate at endline only.  The data were converted to a 6 point scale to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the range of teacher anad head teacher responses at endline. That is followed by a table disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state for midline and endline. The last table is the head teacher data for endline in the same disaggregations.

<br/>

The Teacher Positive School Climate Scale was test for reliability with a cronbach aplha of: `r posschlclimate_alpha_teacher` showing a high reliability.

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher positive school climate, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}
build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_nothingagreatdeal_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TPosSchlClimate_LKRT, EL2HTPosSchlClimate_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher and Head Teacher\nEfficacy to Build a Positive School Climate')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher positive school climate table build, include=TRUE}
TeacherPosSchlClimateTable <- build_median_ME_teacher_function(BMEL2teachersurveylonglocate, MTPosSchlClimateScaleCNV, EL2TPosSchlClimateScaleCNV, "Teacher Efficacy to Build a Positive School Climate")
```

`r TeacherPosSchlClimateTable`

<br/>

```{r head teacher positive school climate table build, include=TRUE}
EL2HTeacherPosSchlClimateTable <- build_median_headteacher_function(EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2HTPosSchlClimateScaleCNV, "Head Teacher Efficacy to Build a Positive School Climate")
```

`r EL2HTeacherPosSchlClimateTable`

<br/>

### Teacher and Head Teacher Perceptions of Student Future Plans and Transitions

Teachers were asked a few different questions on how prepared they believe their students are for the future and which students are most likely to continue their education. The first question that teachers were asked their level of agreement to the following statement: 'I feel my students are adequately prepared to continue with their education.'  Their responses can be seen in the likert figure below.

```{r teacher perception of student preparedness likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=2.5}
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(EL2TStdntFuture_f) %>% count()
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss <- as.data.frame(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss)
numTchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss <- nrow(EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntFuture_f)))

## set column order for stdnt future
col_orderStdntPrprdnss <- c("Question", "Strongly Disagree", "Disagree", "Somewhat Disagree", "Neither Agree nor Disagree", "Somewhat Agree", "Agree", "Strongly Agree")

## get frequency percents by category
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntFuture_f))
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss %>% mutate( per = round((n/numTchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss * 100), 0))
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss$n <- NULL
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength <- nrow(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss)

## add in 0% at the bottom of the pivot for all possible items to ensure all likert levels exist
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss$EL2TStdntFuture_f <- sapply(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss$EL2TStdntFuture_f, as.character)

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+1, 1] <- "Strongly Disagree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+1, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+2, 1] <- "Disagree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+2, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+3, 1] <- "Somewhat Disagree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+3, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+4, 1] <- "Neither Agree nor Disagree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+4, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+5, 1] <- "Somewhat Agree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+5, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+6, 1] <- "Agree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+6, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+7, 1] <- "Strongly Agree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssLength+7, 2] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnsspivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnss %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TStdntFuture_f, values_from = per, values_fn=sum)
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnsspivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnsspivot1 %>% mutate(Question = 'All Teachers')
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnsspivot2 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnsspivot1[,col_orderStdntPrprdnss]

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnsspivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "1%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "2%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "3%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "4%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssPlot <- plot.likert(Question ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnsspivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=4,
            main = "I feel my students are adequately prepared to continue with their education.", col = likertPalette7,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = T,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)), auto.key=list(columns = 2, text=c("Strongly Disagree", "Disagree", "Somewhat Disagree", "Neither Agree nor Disagree", "Somewhat Agree", "Agree", "Strongly Agree")))

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssPlot	
```

<br/>

Next, we look at the teacher perceptions broken out by site type, location type and then by state.

```{r teacher perception of student preparedness site type likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=3}
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(EL2TColocation_f, EL2TStdntFuture_f) %>% count()
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated <- as.data.frame(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated)

## set column order for stdnt future
col_orderStdntPrprdnssColocated <- c("EL2TColocation_f", "Strongly Disagree", "Disagree", "Somewhat Disagree", "Neither Agree nor Disagree", "Somewhat Agree", "Agree", "Strongly Agree")

## get frequency percents by category
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntFuture_f))
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated  %>% mutate(per = round(prop.table(n) * 100, 0))

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated$n <- NULL
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength <- nrow(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated)

## add in 0% at the bottom of the pivot for all possible items to ensure all likert levels exist
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated$EL2TStdntFuture_f <- sapply(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated$EL2TStdntFuture_f, as.character)
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+1, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+1, 2] <- "Strongly Disagree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+1, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+2, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+2, 2] <- "Disagree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+2, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+3, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+3, 2] <- "Somewhat Disagree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+3, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+4, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+4, 2] <- "Neither Agree nor Disagree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+4, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+5, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+5, 2] <- "Somewhat Agree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+5, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+6, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+6, 2] <- "Agree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+6, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+7, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+7, 2] <- "Strongly Agree"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedLength+7, 3] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedpivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocated %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TStdntFuture_f, values_from = per, values_fn=sum)

## set all na's to 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedpivot1[is.na(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedpivot1)] <- 0

## fix column order
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedpivot2 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedpivot1[,col_orderStdntPrprdnssColocated]

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedpivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "1%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "2%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "3%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "4%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedPlot <- plot.likert(EL2TColocation_f ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedpivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=4,
            main = "I feel my students are adequately prepared to continue with their education.\n by Site Type", col = likertPalette7,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = F,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)), auto.key=list(columns = 2,text=c("Strongly Disagree", "Disagree", "Somewhat Disagree", "Neither Agree nor Disagree", "Somewhat Agree", "Agree", "Strongly Agree")))

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdnssColocatedPlot	
```

```{r teacher perception of student preparedness location likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=3}
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)

  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]
```

```{r teacher perception of student preparedness state likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=3.5}
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)

  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks

  DF$perc[DF$perc == "1%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "2%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "3%"] <- ""
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "4%"] <- ""
```

<br/>

The teacher survey were next asked: 'What pathways for further education are available to students attending your school?'  The options for possible pathways included: ABE program, TVET, University Training, No pathways, and Other.  Only one teacher reported 'other pathway' and specified 'Large Projects'.  

```{r teacher available pathways, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=2.5}
EndlineTchrFuturePthwys <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% dplyr::select("ABE Program"=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_01, "TVET"=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_02, "University Training"=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_03, "There are no pathways."=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_04, "Other future pathways"=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_05)

numTchrFuturePthwys <- nrow(EndlineTchrFuturePthwys) 

EndlineTchrFuturePthwys <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrFuturePthwys)
EndlineTchrFuturePthwys <- EndlineTchrFuturePthwys %>% mutate(Base='Endline')

EndlineTchrFuturePthwysAggregate <- aggregate(.~Base, data=EndlineTchrFuturePthwys, FUN=sum)
EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1 <- EndlineTchrFuturePthwysAggregate %>% pivot_longer(!Base, names_to = 'Pathway', values_to = 'Response')
EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1 <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1)
EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1$Pathway <- sapply(EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1$Pathway, as.factor)
EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1$Pathway <- fct_reorder(EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1$Pathway, EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1$Response)
EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1 <- EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1[!(is.na(EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1$Response) | EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1$Response=="0"),]

EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1 <- EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1 %>% mutate(Percent = round(Response/numTchrFuturePthwys*100), 0)
EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1 <- EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1 %>% mutate(Label = paste0(Percent, "%"))

ggplot(data = EndlineTchrFuturePthwyspivot1, aes(x=Pathway, y=Response)) + xlab('Pathways') + ylab('Number of Teachers') + geom_bar(stat="identity", show.legend = FALSE, fill="#31A354") + ggtitle('Teacher Perception of Student Future Pathways Available') + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 45), breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45)) + geom_text(aes(label = Label), hjust = -1.0, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color="#777B7E") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) + guides(fill=guide_legend(reverse=TRUE)) + coord_flip() + labs(colour=NULL, caption="Please note teachers could choose more than one category and therefore the percents do not total to 100.") + theme_classic() + theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.25, linetype = 1), plot.title = element_text(color="black"), axis.title.x = element_text(color="#777B7E"), axis.text.x=element_text(color="#777B7E"))
```

```{r teacher available pathways by Site Type, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=3}
EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteType <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% dplyr::select(EL2TColocation_f, "ABE Program"=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_01, "TVET"=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_02, "University Training"=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_03, "There are no pathways."=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_04, "Other future pathways"=EL2TStdntFuturePthwys_05)

EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot1 <- EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot1[!(is.na(EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot1$Response) | EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot1$Response=="0"),]

EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot2 <- merge(EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot1, EndlineTchrFuturePthwysbyLocateCnt, by.x='EL2TColocation_f', by.y='EL2TColocation_f')

EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot2 <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot2)

EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot2$EL2TColocation_f <- fct_relevel(EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot2$EL2TColocation_f, "BAB Center", "Colocated Site")

ggplot(data = EndlineTchrFuturePthwysSiteTypepivot2, aes(x=EL2TColocation_f, y=Response, fill=Pathway)) + xlab('') + ylab('Number of Teachers') + geom_bar(stat="identity", position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 30), breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30)) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = pvmpalette2) + ggtitle('Teacher Perception of Student Future Pathways Available\n by Site Type') + geom_text(aes(label = Label), position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9), hjust = -0.5, size = 3, fontface = "bold", color="#777B7E") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) + guides(fill=guide_legend(reverse=TRUE)) + coord_flip() + labs(colour=NULL) + theme_classic() + theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.25, linetype = 1), plot.title = element_text(color="black"), axis.title.x = element_text(color="#777B7E"), axis.text.x=element_text(color="#777B7E"))
```

<br/>

Teachers were next asked how many of their students they think will continue their education.  The choices for how many students will continue their education were: "a few(0-24%)", "some(25-49%)", "many(50-74%)", "most(75-100%)".

```{r teacher perception of students continuing education percent likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=2.5}
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(EL2TStdntContinueEd_f) %>% count()
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd <- as.data.frame(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd)
numTchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd <- nrow(EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntContinueEd_f)))

## set column order for stdnt future
col_orderStdntPrprdnss <- c("Question", "a few(0-24%)", "some(25-49%)", "many(50-74%)", "most(75-100%)")

## get frequency percents by category
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntContinueEd_f))
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd %>% mutate( per = round((n/numTchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd * 100), 0))
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd$n <- NULL
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLength <- nrow(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd)

## add in 0% at the bottom of the pivot for all possible items to ensure all likert levels exist
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd$EL2TStdntContinueEd_f <- sapply(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd$EL2TStdntContinueEd_f, as.character)

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLength+1, 1] <- "a few(0-24%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLength+1, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLength+2, 1] <- "some(25-49%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLength+2, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLength+3, 1] <- "many(50-74%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLength+3, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLength+4, 1] <- "most(75-100%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLength+4, 2] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdpivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEd %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TStdntContinueEd_f, values_from = per, values_fn=sum)
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdpivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdpivot1 %>% mutate(Question = 'All Teachers')
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdpivot2 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdpivot1[,col_orderStdntPrprdnss]

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdpivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdPlot <- plot.likert(Question ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdpivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=2.5,
            main = "How many of your students do you think will continue with their education?", col = likertPalette4,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = T,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)))

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdPlot	
```

```{r teacher perception of students continuing education percent by site type likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=3}
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(EL2TColocation_f, EL2TStdntContinueEd_f) %>% count()
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType <- as.data.frame(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType)

## set column order for stdnt future
col_orderStdntPrprdnss <- c("EL2TColocation_f", "a few(0-24%)", "some(25-49%)", "many(50-74%)", "most(75-100%)")

## get frequency percents by category
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntContinueEd_f))
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType %>% group_by(EL2TColocation_f) %>% mutate( per = round((prop.table(n) * 100), 0))
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType$n <- NULL
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength <- nrow(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType)

## add in 0% at the bottom of the pivot for all possible items to ensure all likert levels exist
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType$EL2TStdntContinueEd_f <- sapply(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType$EL2TStdntContinueEd_f, as.character)

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+1, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+1, 2] <- "a few(0-24%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+1, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+2, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+2, 2] <- "some(25-49%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+2, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+3, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+3, 2] <- "many(50-74%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+3, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+4, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+4, 2] <- "most(75-100%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypeLength+4, 3] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypepivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteType %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TStdntContinueEd_f, values_from = per, values_fn=sum)

## set all na's to 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypepivot1[is.na(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypepivot1)] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypepivot2 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypepivot1[,col_orderStdntPrprdnss]

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypepivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypePlot <- plot.likert(EL2TColocation_f ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdSiteTypepivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=2.5,
            main = "How many of your students do you think will continue with their education?\nby Site Type", col = likertPalette4,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = F,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)))
```
