```{r teacher perception of students continuing education percent location likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=3}
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocate[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocateLength+3, 1] <- "IDP"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocate[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocateLength+3, 2] <- "many(50-74%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocate[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocateLength+3, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocate[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocateLength+4, 1] <- "IDP"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocate[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocateLength+4, 2] <- "most(75-100%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocate[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocateLength+4, 3] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocatepivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocate %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TStdntContinueEd_f, values_from = per, values_fn=sum)

## set all na's to 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocatepivot1[is.na(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocatepivot1)] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocatepivot2 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocatepivot1[,col_orderStdntPrprdnss]

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocatepivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocatePlot <- plot.likert(EL2TLocationType_f ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocatepivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=2.5,
            main = "How many of your students do you think will continue with their education?\nby School Location Type", col = likertPalette4,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = F,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)))

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdLocatePlot	
```

```{r teacher perception of students continuing education percent state likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=4.5}
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(EL2TState_f, EL2TStdntContinueEd_f) %>% count()
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState <- as.data.frame(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState)

## set column order for stdnt future
col_orderStdntPrprdnss <- c("EL2TState_f", "a few(0-24%)", "some(25-49%)", "many(50-74%)", "most(75-100%)")

## get frequency percents by category
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntContinueEd_f))
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState %>% group_by(EL2TState_f) %>% mutate( per = round((prop.table(n) * 100), 0))
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState$n <- NULL
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength <- nrow(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState)

## add in 0% at the bottom of the pivot for all possible items to ensure all likert levels exist
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState$EL2TStdntContinueEd_f <- sapply(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState$EL2TStdntContinueEd_f, as.character)

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+1, 1] <- "Benaadir"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+1, 2] <- "a few(0-24%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+1, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+2, 1] <- "Benaadir"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+2, 2] <- "some(25-49%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+2, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+3, 1] <- "Benaadir"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+3, 2] <- "many(50-74%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+3, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+4, 1] <- "Benaadir"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+4, 2] <- "most(75-100%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState[TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStateLength+4, 3] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStatepivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdState %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TStdntContinueEd_f, values_from = per, values_fn=sum)

## set all na's to 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStatepivot1[is.na(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStatepivot1)] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStatepivot2 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStatepivot1[,col_orderStdntPrprdnss]

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStatepivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStatePlot <- plot.likert(EL2TState_f ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStatepivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=2.5,
            main = "How many of your students do you think will continue with their education?\nby School State", col = likertPalette4,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = F,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)))

TchrPrcptnStdntCntngEdStatePlot	
```

<br/>

The percent of students that teachers think will be likely to continue their education was followed up by a question asking teachers what type of students they believe are the **MOST** likely to continue their education.  The types of students that were supplied were put into groups as follows: boys, girls, young children, older children, children with disabilities, and children from certain clans.  As seen in the figure below boys were chosen as the type of students most likely to continue their education as selected by 69% of the teachers, and this was closely followed by 62% of the teachers choosing girls.  Teachers were allowed to choose all categories that apply, and none of the teachers chose the 'Other' category.  The first figure represents all teachers together.  The second figure depicts the teachers broken out by school location type.  The third figure depicts the teachers broken otu by State.  

<br/>

```{r teacher perception of students most likely to continue education, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=2.5}

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEd <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% dplyr::select("boys"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_01, "girls"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_02, "young children"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_03, "older children"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_04, "children with disabilities"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_05, "children from certain clans"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_06)

numTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEd <- nrow(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEd) 

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEd <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEd)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEd <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEd %>% mutate(Base='Endline')

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdAggregate <- aggregate(.~Base, data=EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEd, FUN=sum)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdAggregate %>% pivot_longer(!Base, names_to = 'Reason', values_to = 'Response')
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1 <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1$Reason <- sapply(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1$Reason, as.factor)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1$Reason <- fct_reorder(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1$Reason, EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1$Response)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1[!(is.na(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1$Response) | EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1$Response=="0"),]

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1 %>% mutate(Percent = round(Response/numTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEd*100), 0)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1 %>% mutate(Label = paste0(Percent, "%"))

ggplot(data = EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdpivot1, aes(x=Reason, y=Response)) + xlab('Type of Student') + ylab('Number of Teachers') + geom_bar(stat="identity", show.legend = FALSE, fill="#31A354") + ggtitle('Teacher Perception of Students Most Likely\nto Continue thier Education') + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 40), breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40)) + geom_text(aes(label = Label), hjust = -1.0, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color="#777B7E") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) + guides(fill=guide_legend(reverse=TRUE)) + coord_flip() + labs(colour=NULL, caption="Please note teachers could choose more than one category and therefore the percents do not total to 100.") + theme_classic() + theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.25, linetype = 1), plot.title = element_text(color="black"), axis.title.x = element_text(color="#777B7E"), axis.text.x=element_text(color="#777B7E"))
```

```{r teacher perception of students most likely to continue education by Site Type, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=3}
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocate <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% dplyr::select(EL2TColocation_f, "boys"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_01, "girls"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_02, "young children"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_03, "older children"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_04, "children with disabilities"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_05, "children from certain clans"=EL2TStdntMstLklyCntnEd_06)

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocateCnt <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocate %>% group_by(EL2TColocation_f) %>% count()

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocate <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocate)

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocateAggregate <- aggregate(.~EL2TColocation_f, data=EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocate, FUN=sum)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocateAggregate %>% pivot_longer(!EL2TColocation_f, names_to = 'Student Type', values_to = 'Response')
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1 <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1$`Student Type` <- sapply(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1$`Student Type`, as.factor)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1$`Student Type` <- fct_reorder(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1$`Student Type`, EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1$Response)
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1[!(is.na(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1$Response) | EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1$Response=="0"),]

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2 <- merge(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot1, EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocateCnt, by.x="EL2TColocation_f", by.y="EL2TColocation_f")

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2 %>% mutate(Percent = round((Response/n*100), 0))

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2 %>% mutate(Label = paste0(Percent, "%"))
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2 <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2)

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2$EL2TColocation_f <- fct_relevel(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2$EL2TColocation_f, "BAB Center", "Colocated Site")

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2$`Student Type` <- fct_relevel(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2$`Student Type`, "children with disabilities", "children from certain clans", "older children", "young children", "girls", "boys")

ggplot(data = EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdLocatepivot2, aes(x=EL2TColocation_f, y=Response, fill=`Student Type`)) + xlab('') + ylab('Number of Teachers') + geom_bar(stat="identity", position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 30), breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30)) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = pvmpalette2) + ggtitle('Teacher Perception of Students Most Likely\nto Continue thier Education by Site Type') + geom_text(aes(label = Label), position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9), hjust = -0.5, size = 3, fontface = "bold", color="#777B7E") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) + guides(fill=guide_legend(reverse=TRUE)) + coord_flip() + labs(colour=NULL) + theme_classic() + theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.25, linetype = 1), plot.title = element_text(color="black"), axis.title.x = element_text(color="#777B7E"), axis.text.x=element_text(color="#777B7E"))
```

```{r teacher perception of students most likely to continue education by State, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=3.5}
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot1 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStateAggregate %>% pivot_longer(!EL2TState_f, names_to = 'Reason', values_to = 'Response')

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot1$Reason <- sapply(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot1$Reason, as.factor)

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot1 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot1[!(is.na(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot1$Response) | EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot1$Response=="0"),]

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2 <- merge(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot1, EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStateCnt, by.x='EL2TState_f', by.y='EL2TState_f')

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2 %>% mutate(Percent = round((Response/n)*100), 0)

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2 <- EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2 %>% mutate(Label = paste0(Percent, "%"))
EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2 <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2)

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2$EL2TState_f <- fct_relevel(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2$EL2TState_f, "South-West", "Jubbaland", "Hirshabeelle", "Benaadir")

EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2$Reason <- fct_relevel(EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2$Reason, "children with disabilities", "children from certain clans", "older children", "young children", "girls", "boys")

ggplot(data = EndlineTchrStdntMstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2, aes(x=EL2TState_f, y=Response, fill=Reason)) + xlab('State') + ylab('Number of Teachers') + geom_bar(stat="identity", position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 30), breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30)) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = pvmpalette) + ggtitle('Teacher Perception of Students Most Likely\nto Continue thier Education by State') + geom_text(aes(label = Label), position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9), hjust = -0.5, size = 3, fontface = "bold", color="#777B7E") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) + guides(fill=guide_legend(reverse=TRUE)) + coord_flip() + labs(colour=NULL) + theme_classic() + theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.25, linetype = 1), plot.title = element_text(color="black"), axis.title.x = element_text(color="#777B7E"), axis.text.x=element_text(color="#777B7E"))
```

<br/>

When asked which type of students are **LEAST** likely to continue their education teachers were given the same choices as they were when asked which students are the most likely to continue their education.  The students that teachers believe are least likely to continue their education are first shown by all teachers together, then broken out by school location type, and by state. 

<br/>

```{r teacher perception of students least likely to continue education, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=2.5}

EndlineTchrStdntLstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2$Reason <- fct_relevel(EndlineTchrStdntLstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2$Reason, "children with disabilities", "children from certain clans", "older children", "young children", "girls", "boys")

ggplot(data = EndlineTchrStdntLstLklyCntnEdStatepivot2, aes(x=EL2TState_f, y=Response, fill=Reason)) + xlab('State') + ylab('Number of Teachers') + geom_bar(stat="identity", position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9)) + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 20), breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20)) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = pvmpalette) + ggtitle('Teacher Perception of Students Least Likely\nto Continue thier Education by State') + geom_text(aes(label = Label), position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9), hjust = -0.5, size = 3, fontface = "bold", color="#777B7E") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) + guides(fill=guide_legend(reverse=TRUE)) + coord_flip() + labs(colour=NULL) + theme_classic() + theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.25, linetype = 1), plot.title = element_text(color="black"), axis.title.x = element_text(color="#777B7E"), axis.text.x=element_text(color="#777B7E"))
```

<br/>

Teachers were asked: "How many of your students do you think are academically prepared to move to the next ABE level?"  The choices again were: "a few(0-24%)", "some(25-49%)", "many(50-74%)", "most(75-100%)".

```{r teacher perception of students academic preparation for next ABE level percent likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=2.5}
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(EL2TStdntPrprdNxtABELvl_f) %>% count()
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl <- as.data.frame(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl)
numTchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl <- nrow(EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntPrprdNxtABELvl_f)))

## set column order for stdnt future
col_orderStdntPrprdnssNxtABELvl <- c("Question", "a few(0-24%)", "some(25-49%)", "many(50-74%)", "most(75-100%)")

## get frequency percents by category
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntPrprdNxtABELvl_f))
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl %>% mutate( per = round((n/numTchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl * 100), 0))
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl$n <- NULL
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlLength <- nrow(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl)

## add in 0% at the bottom of the pivot for all possible items to ensure all likert levels exist
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl$EL2TStdntPrprdNxtABELvl_f <- sapply(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl$EL2TStdntPrprdNxtABELvl_f, as.character)

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlLength+1, 1] <- "a few(0-24%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlLength+1, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlLength+2, 1] <- "some(25-49%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlLength+2, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlLength+3, 1] <- "many(50-74%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlLength+3, 2] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlLength+4, 1] <- "most(75-100%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlLength+4, 2] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlpivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvl %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TStdntPrprdNxtABELvl_f, values_from = per, values_fn=sum)
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlpivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlpivot1 %>% mutate(Question = 'All Teachers')
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlpivot2 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlpivot1[,col_orderStdntPrprdnssNxtABELvl]

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlpivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlPlot <- plot.likert(Question ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlpivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=2.5,
```

```{r teacher perception of students academic preparation for next ABE level percent site type likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=3.5}
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType$n <- NULL
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength <- nrow(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType)

## add in 0% at the bottom of the pivot for all possible items to ensure all likert levels exist
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType$EL2TStdntPrprdNxtABELvl_f <- sapply(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType$EL2TStdntPrprdNxtABELvl_f, as.character)

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+1, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+1, 2] <- "a few(0-24%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+1, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+2, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+2, 2] <- "some(25-49%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+2, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+3, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+3, 2] <- "many(50-74%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+3, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+4, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+4, 2] <- "most(75-100%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypeLength+4, 3] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypepivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteType %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TStdntPrprdNxtABELvl_f, values_from = per, values_fn=sum)

## set all na's to 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypepivot1[is.na(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypepivot1)] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypepivot2 <- TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypepivot1[,col_orderStdntPrprdnssNxtABELvl]

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypepivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypePlot <- plot.likert(EL2TColocation_f ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypepivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=2.5,
            main = "How many of your students do you think are\nacademically prepared to move to the next ABE level?\nby Site Type", col = likertPalette4,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = F,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)))

TchrPrcptnStdntPrprdNxtABELvlSiteTypePlot
```

<br/>

Teachers were asked: "How many of your students do you think will pursue learning a trade through TVET or apprenticeship?"  The choices again were: "a few(0-24%)", "some(25-49%)", "many(50-74%)", "most(75-100%)".

```{r teacher perception of students pursuing TVET level percent likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=2.5}

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETpivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETPlot <- plot.likert(Question ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETpivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=2.5,
            main = "How many of your students do you think will pursue\nlearning a trade through TVET or apprenticeship?", col = likertPalette4,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = T,
            ylab = "",

            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)))
```

```{r teacher perception of students pursuing TVET percent site type likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=2.5}
## get frequency percents by category

TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType <- TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType %>% group_by(EL2TColocation_f) %>% mutate( per = round((prop.table(n) * 100), 0))
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType$n <- NULL
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypeLength <- nrow(TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType)

## add in 0% at the bottom of the pivot for all possible items to ensure all likert levels exist
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType$EL2TStdntPrprdTVETApprtcshp_f <- sapply(TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType$EL2TStdntPrprdTVETApprtcshp_f, as.character)

TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypeLength+1, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypeLength+1, 2] <- "a few(0-24%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypeLength+1, 3] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypeLength+2, 2] <- "some(25-49%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypeLength+2, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypeLength+3, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypeLength+3, 2] <- "many(50-74%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteType[TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypeLength+3, 3] <- 0

## set all na's to 0
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypepivot1[is.na(TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypepivot1)] <- 0

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypepivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypePlot <- plot.likert(EL2TColocation_f ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypepivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=2.5,
            main = "How many of your students do you think will pursue\nlearning a trade through TVET or apprenticeship?\nby Site Type", col = likertPalette4,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = F,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)))

TchrPrcptnStdntPursueTVETSiteTypePlot
```

<br/>

Teachers were asked: "How many of your students do you think are academically prepared to continue studying in a formal school program?"  The choices again were: "a few(0-24%)", "some(25-49%)", "many(50-74%)", "most(75-100%)".

```{r teacher perception of students continue formal primary likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=2.5}
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(EL2TStdntPrprdFrmlSchlPrgm_f) %>% count()
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP <- as.data.frame(TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP)
numTchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP <- nrow(EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntPrprdFrmlSchlPrgm_f)))

## set column order for stdnt future

## get frequency percents by category

    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK

  # change all "0%" to blanks
```

```{r teacher perception of students continue formal primary site type likert, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.8, fig.height=2.5}
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP <- as.data.frame(TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP)

## set column order for stdnt future
col_orderStdntCntnFP <- c("EL2TColocation_f", "a few(0-24%)", "some(25-49%)", "many(50-74%)", "most(75-100%)")

## get frequency percents by category
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP %>% filter(!is.na(EL2TStdntPrprdFrmlSchlPrgm_f))
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP %>% group_by(EL2TColocation_f) %>% mutate( per = round((prop.table(n) * 100), 0))
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP$n <- NULL
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength <- nrow(TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP)

## add in 0% at the bottom of the pivot for all possible items to ensure all likert levels exist
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP$EL2TStdntPrprdFrmlSchlPrgm_f <- sapply(TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP$EL2TStdntPrprdFrmlSchlPrgm_f, as.character)

TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+1, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+1, 2] <- "a few(0-24%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+1, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+2, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+2, 2] <- "some(25-49%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+2, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+3, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+3, 2] <- "many(50-74%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+3, 3] <- 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+4, 1] <- "BAB Center"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+4, 2] <- "most(75-100%)"
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP[TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPLength+4, 3] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPpivot1 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFP %>% pivot_wider(names_from = EL2TStdntPrprdFrmlSchlPrgm_f, values_from = per, values_fn=sum)

## set all na's to 0
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPpivot1[is.na(TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPpivot1)] <- 0

TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPpivot2 <- TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPpivot1[,col_orderStdntCntnFP]

origNames = colnames(TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPpivot2)
# define a custom panel function
myPanelFunc <- function(...){
  panel.likert(...)
  vals <- list(...)
  DF <- data.frame(x=vals$x, y=vals$y, groups=vals$groups)

  ### some convoluted calculations here...
  grps <- as.character(DF$groups)
  for(i in 1:length(origNames)){
    grps <- sub(paste0('^',origNames[i]),i,grps)
  }

  DF <- DF[order(DF$y,grps),]

  DF$correctX <- ave(DF$x,DF$y,FUN=function(x){
    x[x < 0] <- rev(cumsum(rev(x[x < 0]))) - x[x < 0]/2
    x[x > 0] <- cumsum(x[x > 0]) - x[x > 0]/2
    return(x)
  })

  subs <- sub(' Positive$','',DF$groups)
  collapse <- subs[-1] == subs[-length(subs)] & DF$y[-1] == DF$y[-length(DF$y)]
  DF$abs <- abs(DF$x)
  DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- DF$abs[c(collapse,FALSE)] + DF$abs[c(FALSE,collapse)]
  DF$correctX[c(collapse,FALSE)] <- 0
  DF <- DF[c(TRUE,!collapse),]

  DF$perc <- round(ave(DF$abs,DF$y,FUN=function(x){x/sum(x) * 100}), 0)

  ## Here goes 6 lines that have been changes - AK
  # here we modify the column with labels a bit:
  DF$perc <- paste0(DF$perc,'%')
  # change all "0%" to blanks
  DF$perc[DF$perc == "0%"] <- ""
  # the argument label is a bit modified too
  panel.text(x=DF$correctX, y=DF$y, label=DF$perc, cex=0.8)
}	
	
TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPPlot <- plot.likert(EL2TColocation_f ~ ., TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPpivot2,
            as.percent=TRUE, ReferenceZero=2.5,
            main = "How many of your students do you think are academically\nprepared to continue studying in a formal school program?\nby Site Type", col = likertPalette4,
            xlab = "",  
            positive.order = F,
            ylab = "",    
            key.border.white=T,
            panel=myPanelFunc,  
            rightAxis=F, par.settings.in =list(axis.line = list(col = 0)))

TchrPrcptnStdntCntnFPPlot	
```

### Teacher and Head Teacher Perceptions of Community Engagement/Acceptance

```{r teacher and head teacher perception of CEC engagement and acceptance alpha, include=FALSE}

cec_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Perception of Community Acceptance or Engagement TPCES Scale Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

cec_alpha_teacher <- round(cec_alpha_teacher, 2)
```

<br/>

Teachers and Head Teachers were asked their level of agreement to a set of 4 statements on how involved CECs (Communication Education Committee's) are in education programs and services in their schools. When tested for reliability, the community engagement scale showed a high level of reliability with a cronbach alpha of: `r  cec_alpha_teacher`. With a high level of reliability the scale is shown below as a scale. The 4 items in the community engagement scale are as follows: 

 Teachers and Head Teachers measured the statements on the following 7 point scale: 0-strongly disagree, 1-disagree, 2-somewhat disagree, 3-neither agree nor disagree, 4-somewhat agree, 5-agree, 6-strongly agree
 
<br/>

* My school creates opportunities for community members to have input into what programs and services are provided.
* I am able to inform my school's leadership (head teacher) of what community members would like to see the school offer.
* My school encourages students to participate in community activities.
* My school intentionally links students to community activities.

<br/>

All items were collected on a 7 point Likert scale (0-strongly disagree, 1-disagree, 2-somewhat disagree, 3-neither agree nor disagree, 4-somewhat agree, 5-agree, 6-strongly agree). The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison of teachers and head teachers at endline.  The likert figure is followed by a table of teacher medians for midline and endline disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. The final table contains the head teacher medians for endline only because this data was only collected at endline for head teachers.

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher CEC engagement, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}

build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_agreedisagree_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TPCESCNV_LKRT, EL2HTPCESCNV_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher and Head Teacher Efficacy of Community Engagement and Acceptance')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher efficacy of CEC engagement table build, include=TRUE}
TeacherCECTable <- build_median_ME_teacher_function(BMEL2teachersurveylonglocate, MTPCESScaleCNV, EL2TPCESScaleCNV, "Efficacy of Community Engagement and Acceptance")
```

`r TeacherCECTable`

<br/>

```{r head teacher efficacy of CEC engagement table build, include=TRUE}
EL2HTeacherCECTable <- build_median_headteacher_function(EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2HTPCESScaleCNV, "Efficacy of Community Engagement and Acceptance")
```

`r EL2HTeacherCECTable`

<br/>


### Teacher and Head Teacher Self-Efficacy to Enlist Parental Involvement in the classroom/the school

```{r teacher and head teacher parental invovlement alpha, include=FALSE}

parental_alpha_teacher <- TeacherScaleAlphaScoresdf %>% filter(Scale=='Teacher Parental Scale Converted') %>% dplyr::select(`Std Alpha`)

parental_alpha_teacher <- round(parental_alpha_teacher, 2)
```
<br/>

Teachers and Head Teachers were asked a set of 3 questions on their ability to get the parents of their students involved in school activities. When tested for reliability, the parental involvement scale showed a high level of reliability with a cronbach alpha of: `r  parental_alpha_teacher`. With a high level of reliability the scale is shown below as a scale. The three items in the parental scale are as follows: 

<br/>

* How much can you do to get parents to become involved in school activities?  
* How much can you assist parents in helping their children do well in school?  
* How much can you do to make parents feel comfortable coming to school?  

<br/>

All items were collected on a 5 point Likert scale (0-nothing, 1-very little, 2-some influence, 3-quite a bit, 4-a great deal). The data were converted to a 6 point scale here to make comparisons to the other scales. The data values are first displayed here in a likert graph showing the comparison of teachers and head teachers at endline.  The likert figure is followed by a table of teacher medians for midline and endline disaggregated by school level and teacher demographics: school site type, school location type, school funding type, teacher gender, teacher age group, school state. The final table contains the head teacher medians for endline only because this data was only collected at endline for head teachers.

<br/>

```{r call simple likert for teacher and head teacher parental involvement, include=TRUE, fig.height=2.8, fig.width=7.8}

build_teacherHeadteacherlikert_nothingagreatdeal_function(EL2teachersurveylonglocate, EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2TParentalCNV_LKRT, EL2HTParentalCNV_LKRT, 'Endline Teacher and Head Teacher Perception of Parental Involvement')
```

<br/>

```{r teacher efficacy of parental involvement table build, include=TRUE}
TeacherParentalTable <- build_median_ME_teacher_function(BMEL2teachersurveylonglocate, MTParentalScaleCNV, EL2TParentalScaleCNV, "Efficacy of Parental Invovlement")
```

`r TeacherParentalTable`

<br/>

<br/>

```{r head teacher efficacy of parental involvement table build, include=TRUE}
EL2HTeacherParentalTable <- build_median_headteacher_function(EL2headteachersurveylonglocate, EL2HTParentalScaleCNV, "Efficacy of Parental Involvement")
```

`r EL2HTeacherIntSupportTable`

<br/>


### Teacher and Head Teacher Perception of Community Education Committee (CEC) Involvement in their School

Teachers and Head Teachers were asked if their school has a CEC and if yes, what type of involvement the CEC has in their school.  

<br/>

All types of CEC involvement items were asked with a yes/no response and were independent of each other.

<br/>

```{r endline teacher perception of CEC involvement, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5,   fig.height=4.0}

SchoolNoCEC <- EL2headteachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% filter(EL2HTECE_f=='No') %>% group_by(EL2HTState, EL2HTSchoolName, EL2HTECE_f) %>% count()

EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% dplyr::select("no help"=EL2TSchoolCEC_01, "building construction or repair"=EL2TSchoolCEC_02, "school safety"=EL2TSchoolCEC_03, "materials & supplies"=EL2TSchoolCEC_04, "drinking water"=EL2TSchoolCEC_05, "school progress plan"=EL2TSchoolCEC_06, "follow up on student absences"=EL2TSchoolCEC_07, "follow up on teacher absences"=EL2TSchoolCEC_08, "financial support"=EL2TSchoolCEC_09)

numTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- nrow(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve) 

EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve)
EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve %>% mutate(Base='Teachers')

EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolveAggregate <- aggregate(.~Base, data=EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve, FUN=sum)
EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolveAggregate %>% pivot_longer(!Base, names_to = 'Reason', values_to = 'Response')
EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1)
EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason <- sapply(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason, as.factor)
EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason <- fct_reorder(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason, EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Response)
EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1[!(is.na(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Response) | EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Response=="0"),]

EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 %>% mutate(Percent = round(Response/numTchrPrcptCECInvolve*100), 0)
EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 %>% mutate(Label = paste0(Percent, "%"))

EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- EL2headteachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% dplyr::select("no help"=EL2HTECE_01, "building construction or repair"=EL2HTECE_02, "school safety"=EL2HTECE_03, "materials & supplies"=EL2HTECE_04, "drinking water"=EL2HTECE_05, "school progress plan"=EL2HTECE_06, "follow up on student absences"=EL2HTECE_07, "follow up on teacher absences"=EL2HTECE_08, "financial support"=EL2HTECE_09)

numHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- nrow(EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve) 

EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- as.data.frame(EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve)
EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve %>% mutate(Base='Head Teachers')

EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolveAggregate <- aggregate(.~Base, data=EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve, FUN=sum)
EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolveAggregate %>% pivot_longer(!Base, names_to = 'Reason', values_to = 'Response')
EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- as.data.frame(EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1)
EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason <- sapply(EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason, as.factor)
EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason <- fct_reorder(EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason, EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Response)
EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1[!(is.na(EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Response) | EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Response=="0"),]

EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 %>% mutate(Percent = round(Response/numHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve*100), 0)
EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 %>% mutate(Label = paste0(Percent, "%"))

EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined <- rbind(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1, EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1)

EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Reason <- fct_relevel(EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Reason, "no help", "financial support", "drinking water", "materials & supplies", "follow up on teacher absences", "school progress plan", "building construction or repair", "school safety", "follow up on student absences")

EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined <- as.data.frame(EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined)
EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Base <- sapply(EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Base, as.factor)
EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Base <- fct_relevel(EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Base, "Teachers", "Head Teachers")


ggplot(data = EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined, aes(x=Base,  y=Response, fill=Reason)) + ylab('Number of Teachers/Head Teachers') + xlab('') + geom_bar(stat="identity", position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9)) + ggtitle('Endline Head Teachers and Teachers Perceptions\nof CEC Involvement in their School') + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 45), breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45)) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = pvmboxplotpalette) + geom_text(aes(label = Label), position = position_dodge2(preserve = "single", width=0.9), hjust = -0.5, size = 3, fontface = "bold", color="#777B7E") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) + guides(fill=guide_legend(reverse=TRUE)) + coord_flip() + labs(colour=NULL) + theme_classic() + theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.25, linetype = 1), plot.title = element_text(color="black"), axis.title.x = element_text(color="#777B7E"), axis.text.x=element_text(color="#777B7E"))
```

```{r head teacher perception of CEC involvement from baseline to endline, include=FALSE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=5.0}

EndlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian <- EL2headteachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(EL2HTState_f, EL2HTSchoolName, EL2HTECESum) %>% count()
EndlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian <- as.data.frame(EndlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian)
EndlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian$n <- NULL
colnames(EndlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian) <- c("State", "School", "Endline CEC Sum")

MidlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian <- MheadteachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(MHTState_f, MHTSchoolName, MHTECESum) %>% count()
MidlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian <- as.data.frame(MidlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian)
MidlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian$n <- NULL
colnames(MidlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian) <- c("State", "School", "Midline CEC Sum")

BaselineHTECESumbySchoolMedian <- BheadteachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% group_by(BHTState_f, BHTSchoolName, BHTECESum) %>% count()
BaselineHTECESumbySchoolMedian <- as.data.frame(BaselineHTECESumbySchoolMedian)
BaselineHTECESumbySchoolMedian$n <- NULL
colnames(BaselineHTECESumbySchoolMedian) <- c("State", "School", "Baseline CEC Sum")

BMHTECESumbySchoolCombo <- merge(BaselineHTECESumbySchoolMedian, MidlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian, by.x=c("State", "School"), by.y=c("State", "School"), all.x=TRUE, all.y=TRUE)

BMEL2HTCECSumbySchoolCombo <- merge(BMHTECESumbySchoolCombo, EndlineHTECESumbySchoolMedian, by.x=c("State", "School"), by.y=c("State", "School"), all.x=TRUE, all.y=TRUE)




```

```{r endline teacher and head teacher combined perception of CEC involvement, include=TRUE, fig.width=7.5,   fig.height=4.0}

SchoolNoCEC <- EL2headteachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% filter(EL2HTECE_f=='No') %>% group_by(EL2HTState, EL2HTSchoolName, EL2HTECE_f) %>% count()

EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- EL2teachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% filter(EL2TSchoolCEC_f=='Yes') %>% dplyr::select("no help"=EL2TSchoolCEC_01, "building construction or repair"=EL2TSchoolCEC_02, "school safety"=EL2TSchoolCEC_03, "materials & supplies"=EL2TSchoolCEC_04, "drinking water"=EL2TSchoolCEC_05, "school progress plan"=EL2TSchoolCEC_06, "follow up on student absences"=EL2TSchoolCEC_07, "follow up on teacher absences"=EL2TSchoolCEC_08, "financial support"=EL2TSchoolCEC_09)

numTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- nrow(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve) 

EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve)
EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve %>% mutate(Base='Teachers and Head Teachers')

EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- EL2headteachersurveylonglocateSansOutliers %>% filter(EL2HTECE_f=='Yes') %>% dplyr::select("no help"=EL2HTECE_01, "building construction or repair"=EL2HTECE_02, "school safety"=EL2HTECE_03, "materials & supplies"=EL2HTECE_04, "drinking water"=EL2HTECE_05, "school progress plan"=EL2HTECE_06, "follow up on student absences"=EL2HTECE_07, "follow up on teacher absences"=EL2HTECE_08, "financial support"=EL2HTECE_09)

numHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- nrow(EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve) 
TotalnumTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- numTchrPrcptCECInvolve + numHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve

EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- as.data.frame(EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve)
EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve %>% mutate(Base='Teachers and Head Teachers')

EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve <- rbind(EndlineTchrPrcptCECInvolve, EndlineHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve)

EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolveAggregate <- aggregate(.~Base, data=EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve, FUN=sum)
EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolveAggregate %>% pivot_longer(!Base, names_to = 'Reason', values_to = 'Response')
EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- as.data.frame(EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1)
EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason <- sapply(EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason, as.factor)
EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason <- fct_reorder(EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Reason, EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Response)
EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1[!(is.na(EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Response) | EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1$Response=="0"),]

EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 <- EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 %>% mutate(Percent = round(Response/TotalnumTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolve*100), 0)
EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined <- EndlineTchrHeadTchrPrcptCECInvolvepivot1 %>% mutate(Label = paste0(Percent, "%"))

EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Reason <- fct_relevel(EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Reason, "no help", "financial support", "drinking water", "materials & supplies", "follow up on teacher absences", "school progress plan", "building construction or repair", "school safety", "follow up on student absences")

EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined <- as.data.frame(EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined)
EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Base <- sapply(EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined$Base, as.factor)

ggplot(data = EndlinePrcptCECInvolveCombined, aes(x=Reason,  y=Response)) + ylab('Number of Teachers/Head Teachers') + xlab('') + geom_bar(stat="identity", show.legend = FALSE, fill="#31A354") + ggtitle('Teacher and Head Teacher Perceptions\nof CEC Involvement in their School') + scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 55), breaks = c(0, 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35, 40, 45, 50, 55)) + scale_fill_brewer(palette = pvmboxplotpalette) + geom_text(aes(label = Label), hjust = -1.0, size = 3.5, fontface = "bold", color="#777B7E") + scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 35)) + guides(fill=guide_legend(reverse=TRUE)) + coord_flip() + labs(colour=NULL) + theme_classic() + theme(panel.grid.major.x = element_line(color = "gray", size = 0.25, linetype = 1), plot.title = element_text(color="black"), axis.title.x = element_text(color="#777B7E"), axis.text.x=element_text(color="#777B7E"))
```

\newpage


### Teacher and Head Teacher Perception of Student Absence and Retention

Teachers were asked why some of their students fail to come to school at Endline?

```{r Teacher Reason for missing school at endline, include = TRUE, fig.width=7.5, fig.height=3}
TReasontable <- EL2teachersurveylonglocate %>% dplyr::select("Base"=EL2TTimePeriod, "Unable to learn" = EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_01, "Distance" = EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_02, "No School Compound" = EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_03, "No sports ground" = EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_04, "No washroom"=EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_05, "School has no board, desks, nor windows"=EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_06, "No carpeting"=EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_08, "Student bullying"=EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_09, "No books"=EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_10, "No pen and paper"=EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_11, "Unsafe getting to school"=EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_12, "Other"=EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_13, "Unknown"=EL2TStdntAbstainSchl_14 )

TReasontable <- aggregate(.~Base,data=TReasontable,FUN=sum)
TReasontablepivot1 <- TReasontable %>% pivot_longer(!Base, names_to = 'Reason', values_to = 'Response')
TReasontablepivot1 <- as.data.frame(TReasontablepivot1)
TReasontablepivot1$Reason <- sapply(TReasontablepivot1$Reason, as.factor)
TReasontablepivot1$Reason <- fct_relevel(TReasontablepivot1$Reason, "Unknown", "Student bullying", "No carpeting", "Unsafe getting to school", "No pen and paper",  "School has no board, desks, nor windows", "No School Compound", "No washroom", "No books", "No sports ground", "Unable to learn", "Other", "Distance")

##TReasontablepivot1$Reason <- fct_rev(TReasontablepivot1$Reason)

ggplot(data = TReasontablepivot1, aes(x=Reason, y=Response, fill=Base)) + xlab(NULL) + ylab('Number of Teachers') + scale_y_continuous(breaks=c(0,5,10,15,20,25,30), limits=c(0, 30)) + 
	  geom_bar(stat="identity", position=position_dodge(), color="grey", fill="#31A354") + ggtitle('Teacher Reason for Student Absence') + guides(fill = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) +
	  coord_flip() +
	  labs(colour = NULL) +
    theme_classic()

```



<br/>



## Appendix A.1 Data for Dialect Case Study

#### Demographics by Dialect

The demographics below represent the students that were retained in the longitudinal cohort.  They  began as BAB Level 1 students or Formal Primary grade 1 and 2 in year 1 and proceeded as Level 2 students or Formal Primary grade 3 students in year 2.

<br/>

```{r Demographics by dialect, include=TRUE}
ColocationbyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(Colocated_f)
## add percent
ColocationbyDialect <- ColocationbyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add labels
ColocationbyDialect <- ColocationbyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(Colocated_f,  " (", specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%)' ))
## convert to dataframe
ColocationbyDialect <- as.data.frame(ColocationbyDialect)
ColocationbyDialect <- ColocationbyDialect %>% filter(Colocated_f=='Yes')
ColocationbyDialect2 <- ColocationbyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
ColocationbyDialectPivot <- ColocationbyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
ColocationbyDialectPivot <- ColocationbyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Colocated")

IDPFamilybyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(IDPFamily_f)
## add percent
IDPFamilybyDialect <- IDPFamilybyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add labels
IDPFamilybyDialect <- IDPFamilybyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0('Yes',  " (", specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%)' ))
## convert to dataframe
IDPFamilybyDialect <- as.data.frame(IDPFamilybyDialect)
IDPFamilybyDialect <- IDPFamilybyDialect %>% filter(IDPFamily_f=='IDP Family')
IDPFamilybyDialect2 <- IDPFamilybyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
IDPFamilybyDialectPivot <- IDPFamilybyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
IDPFamilybyDialectPivot <- IDPFamilybyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="IDP Family")

GenderbyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(Gender_f)
## add percent
GenderbyDialect <- GenderbyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add percent
GenderbyDialect <- GenderbyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(Gender_f, " (", specify_decimal(per, sigdig2),  '%)'))
## convert to dataframe
GenderbyDialect <- as.data.frame(GenderbyDialect)
GenderbyDialect <- GenderbyDialect %>% arrange(Languages2_f, n)
GenderbyDialect <- GenderbyDialect[-c(1,3,5),]
GenderbyDialect2 <- GenderbyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
GenderbyDialectPivot <- GenderbyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
GenderbyDialectPivot <- GenderbyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Predominant Gender")

MedianAgebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% summarise(median=median(StudentAge))
MedianAgebyDialectPivot <- MedianAgebyDialect %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = median)
MedianAgebyDialectPivot <- MedianAgebyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Median Age")

SESbyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(EL2SES_Sum_f)
## add percent
SESbyDialect <- SESbyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add labels
SESbyDialect <- SESbyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(EL2SES_Sum_f,  " (", specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%)' ))
## convert to dataframe
SESbyDialect <- as.data.frame(SESbyDialect)
SESbyDialect <- SESbyDialect %>% arrange(Languages2_f, n)
SESbyDialect <- SESbyDialect[-c(1,2,4,5,7,8),]
SESbyDialect2 <- SESbyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
SESbyDialectPivot <- SESbyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
SESbyDialectPivot <- SESbyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Predominant SES")

AttendOtherPriorbyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(AttendOtherPrior_f)
AttendOtherPriorbyDialect <- AttendOtherPriorbyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
AttendOtherPriorbyDialect <- AttendOtherPriorbyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%'))
AttendOtherPriorbyDialect <- as.data.frame(AttendOtherPriorbyDialect)
AttendOtherPriorbyDialect <- AttendOtherPriorbyDialect %>% filter(AttendOtherPrior_f=='Yes')
AttendOtherPriorbyDialect2 <- AttendOtherPriorbyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
AttendOtherPriorbyDialect2Pivot <- AttendOtherPriorbyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
AttendOtherPriorbyDialect2Pivot <- AttendOtherPriorbyDialect2Pivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Prior Ed")

MaternalEdbyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(MotherLiteracy_f)
MaternalEdbyDialect <- MaternalEdbyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
MaternalEdbyDialect <- MaternalEdbyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%'))
MaternalEdbyDialect <- as.data.frame(MaternalEdbyDialect)
MaternalEdbyDialect <- MaternalEdbyDialect %>% filter(MotherLiteracy_f=='Yes')
MaternalEdbyDialect2 <- MaternalEdbyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
MaternalEdbyDialect2Pivot <- MaternalEdbyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
MaternalEdbyDialect2Pivot <- MaternalEdbyDialect2Pivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Maternal Ed")

PaternalEdbyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(FatherLiteracy_f)
## add percent
PaternalEdbyDialect <- PaternalEdbyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add labels
PaternalEdbyDialect <- PaternalEdbyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%' ))
## convert to dataframe
PaternalEdbyDialect <- as.data.frame(PaternalEdbyDialect)
PaternalEdbyDialect <- PaternalEdbyDialect %>% filter(FatherLiteracy_f=='Yes')
PaternalEdbyDialect2 <- PaternalEdbyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
PaternalEdbyDialectPivot <- PaternalEdbyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
PaternalEdbyDialectPivot <- PaternalEdbyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Paternal Ed")

StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyMatchedQuadsSansOutlier %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(StudentLivesWithOtherDesc)
StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect <- StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect <- StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%'))
StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect <- as.data.frame(StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect)
StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect <- StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect %>% filter(StudentLivesWithOtherDesc=='Yes')
StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect2 <- StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect2Pivot <- StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect2Pivot <- StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect2Pivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Lives With Other")

DialectTable <- rbind(ColocationbyDialectPivot, IDPFamilybyDialectPivot, GenderbyDialectPivot, MedianAgebyDialectPivot, SESbyDialectPivot, AttendOtherPriorbyDialect2Pivot, MaternalEdbyDialect2Pivot, PaternalEdbyDialectPivot, StudentLivesWOtherbyDialect2Pivot)

DialectTable2 <- DialectTable[, c(4, 1, 2, 3)]
DialectTable2 <- flextable(DialectTable2)
DialectTable2 <- autofit(DialectTable2)
DialectTable2 <- add_header_row(DialectTable2, values = c("  ", "Dialect"), colwidths = c(1,3))
DialectTable2 <- bold(DialectTable2, part = "header", bold = TRUE) 
DialectTable2 <- hline_top(DialectTable2, j = NULL, border = NULL, part = "header")
DialectTable2 <- vline(DialectTable2, j=1, part="all")
DialectTable2 <- border_inner_h(DialectTable2, border = NULL, part = "body")
```

<br/>

`r DialectTable2`

<br/>

The demographics in the next table represent all students except for outliers at baseline.

```{r Demographics by dialect at baseline, include=TRUE}
ColocationBaselinebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyYr1Begin %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(Colocated_f)
## add percent
ColocationBaselinebyDialect <- ColocationBaselinebyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add labels
ColocationBaselinebyDialect <- ColocationBaselinebyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(Colocated_f,  " (", specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%)' ))
## convert to dataframe
ColocationBaselinebyDialect <- as.data.frame(ColocationBaselinebyDialect)
ColocationBaselinebyDialect <- ColocationBaselinebyDialect %>% filter(Colocated_f=='Yes')
ColocationBaselinebyDialect2 <- ColocationBaselinebyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
ColocationBaselinebyDialectPivot <- ColocationBaselinebyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
ColocationBaselinebyDialectPivot <- ColocationBaselinebyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Colocated")

IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyYr1Begin %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(IDPFamily_f)
## add percent
IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect <- IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add labels
IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect <- IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0('Yes',  " (", specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%)' ))
## convert to dataframe
IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect <- as.data.frame(IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect)
IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect <- IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect %>% filter(IDPFamily_f=='IDP Family')
IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect2 <- IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialectPivot <- IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialectPivot <- IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="IDP Family")

GenderBaselinebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyYr1Begin %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(Gender_f)
## add percent
GenderBaselinebyDialect <- GenderBaselinebyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add percent
GenderBaselinebyDialect <- GenderBaselinebyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(Gender_f, " (", specify_decimal(per, sigdig2),  '%)'))
## convert to dataframe
GenderBaselinebyDialect <- as.data.frame(GenderBaselinebyDialect)
GenderBaselinebyDialect <- GenderBaselinebyDialect %>% arrange(Languages2_f, n)
GenderBaselinebyDialect <- GenderBaselinebyDialect[-c(1,3,5),]
GenderBaselinebyDialect2 <- GenderBaselinebyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
GenderBaselinebyDialectPivot <- GenderBaselinebyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
GenderBaselinebyDialectPivot <- GenderBaselinebyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Predominant Gender")

MedianAgeBaselinebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyYr1Begin %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% summarise(median=median(StudentAge))
MedianAgeBaselinebyDialectPivot <- MedianAgeBaselinebyDialect %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = median)
MedianAgeBaselinebyDialectPivot <- MedianAgeBaselinebyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Median Age")

SESBaselinebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyYr1Begin %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(SES_Sum_f)
## add percent
SESBaselinebyDialect <- SESBaselinebyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add labels
SESBaselinebyDialect <- SESBaselinebyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(SES_Sum_f,  " (", specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%)' ))
## convert to dataframe
SESBaselinebyDialect <- as.data.frame(SESBaselinebyDialect)
SESBaselinebyDialect <- SESBaselinebyDialect %>% filter(!is.na(SES_Sum_f))
SESBaselinebyDialect <- SESBaselinebyDialect %>% arrange(Languages2_f, n)
SESBaselinebyDialect <- SESBaselinebyDialect[-c(1,2,3,5,6,7,9,10),]
SESBaselinebyDialect2 <- SESBaselinebyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
SESBaselinebyDialectPivot <- SESBaselinebyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
SESBaselinebyDialectPivot <- SESBaselinebyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Predominant SES")

AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyYr1Begin %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(AttendOtherPrior_f)
AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect <- AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect <- AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%'))
AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect <- as.data.frame(AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect)
AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect <- AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect %>% filter(AttendOtherPrior_f=='Yes')
AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect2 <- AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect2Pivot <- AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect2Pivot <- AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect2Pivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Prior Ed")

MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyYr1Begin %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(MotherLiteracy_f)
MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%'))
MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- as.data.frame(MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect)
MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect %>% filter(MotherLiteracy_f=='Yes')
MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect2 <- MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect2Pivot <- MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect2Pivot <- MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect2Pivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Maternal Ed")

PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyYr1Begin %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(FatherLiteracy_f)
## add percent
PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
## add labels
PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%' ))
## convert to dataframe
PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- as.data.frame(PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect)
PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect <- PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect %>% filter(FatherLiteracy_f=='Yes')
PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect2 <- PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
PaternalEdBaselinebyDialectPivot <- PaternalEdBaselinebyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
PaternalEdBaselinebyDialectPivot <- PaternalEdBaselinebyDialectPivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Paternal Ed")

StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect <- EndlinestudentsurveyYr1Begin %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% count(StudentLivesWithOtherDesc)
StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect <- StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect %>% group_by(Languages2_f) %>% mutate(per = round((n/sum(n))*100, sigdig2))
StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect <- StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect %>% mutate(label = paste0(specify_decimal(per, sigdig2), '%'))
StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect <- as.data.frame(StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect)
StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect <- StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect %>% filter(StudentLivesWithOtherDesc=='Yes')
StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect2 <- StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect %>% dplyr::select(Languages2_f, label)
StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect2Pivot <- StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect2 %>% pivot_wider(names_from = Languages2_f, values_from = label)
StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect2Pivot <- StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect2Pivot %>% mutate(`Student Characteristic`="Lives With Other")

DialectBaselineTable <- rbind(ColocationBaselinebyDialectPivot, IDPFamilyBaselinebyDialectPivot, GenderBaselinebyDialectPivot, MedianAgeBaselinebyDialectPivot, SESBaselinebyDialectPivot, AttendOtherPriorBaselinebyDialect2Pivot, MaternalEdBaselinebyDialect2Pivot, PaternalEdBaselinebyDialectPivot, StudentLivesWOtherBaselinebyDialect2Pivot)

DialectBaselineTable2 <- DialectBaselineTable[, c(4, 1, 2, 3)]
DialectBaselineTable2 <- flextable(DialectBaselineTable2)
DialectBaselineTable2 <- autofit(DialectBaselineTable2)
DialectBaselineTable2 <- add_header_row(DialectBaselineTable2, values = c("  ", "Dialect"), colwidths = c(1,3))
DialectBaselineTable2 <- bold(DialectBaselineTable2, part = "header", bold = TRUE) 
DialectBaselineTable2 <- hline_top(DialectBaselineTable2, j = NULL, border = NULL, part = "header")
DialectBaselineTable2 <- vline(DialectBaselineTable2, j=1, part="all")
DialectBaselineTable2 <- border_inner_h(DialectBaselineTable2, border = NULL, part = "body")
```

`r DialectBaselineTable2`
